outer_lands.notes: No single-room smoke test is accepted as proof of the complete dungeon lifecycle.

steps: Transform a bookshelf with a focus-free wand
Observe the 0.25-size Thaumonomicon drop hover without falling
Trigger a nearby non-destructive explosion and collect it
tests.expected: The custom SpecialItem survives the explosion, remains collectible and does not climb or sink.

steps: Complete an alchemical crucible recipe
Observe every output stack ejected at y+0.71
Collect the result after its ten-tick delay
tests.expected: The first stack rises vertically at 0.1 and additional stacks receive only the original tiny x/z jitter.

steps: Generate an Outer Lands key room
Leave the permanent key item loaded beyond five minutes and save/reload
Pick it up and verify progression
tests.expected: The custom PermanentItem never despawns, survives reload and remains normally collectible.

steps: Kill an Eldritch boss or Cultist Portal
Observe the special relic reward during death effects
Cause an explosion beside the reward and pick it up
tests.expected: The named TC4 boss reward uses SpecialItem, has a ten-tick pickup delay and cannot be destroyed by explosion damage.

steps: Fill the inventory and use Equal Trade so a drop cannot enter it
Observe the custom FollowingItem curve toward the player
Move behind a wall and wait until it reaches the target radius
tests.expected: The item homes for up to the original 20-step divisor, emits its colored trail, then restores normal collision/gravity and can be collected.

steps: Spawn all three custom item entity types on a dedicated server
Join with two clients and compare item stacks, target and particle type
Save/reload while a FollowingItem is in flight
tests.expected: Forge spawn packets and SynchedEntityData show the same stack/target/type to both clients; NBT restores permanent and following state.

steps: Inspect the Axe of the Stream in survival
Compare durability, mining speed, enchantability and repair material
Hold right click and observe the bow-style use pose
tests.expected: The axe has 1500 durability, tier 3, speed 10, +3 material damage, enchantability 18, rare rarity, repairs with thaumium and can be held in use for 72000 ticks.

steps: Drop items at several distances up to ten blocks
Hold use with the Axe of the Stream
Measure velocity and release the use action
tests.expected: Loose items accelerate toward the player by 0.3 per tick with every axis clamped to +/-0.35 and stop receiving acceleration immediately after release.

steps: Create an active Following Item and a normal loose item nearby
Hold use with the axe on a dedicated server
Compare motion and blue stream particles on two clients
tests.expected: The active Following Item keeps its own homing path; ordinary loose items are pulled and emit the original blue stream effect.

steps: Build connected same-log trees with branches and nearby different log species
Break the base while not sneaking
Repeat near the original 24/48/24 search bounds
tests.expected: Each action harvests one greedily selected furthest connected log of the same block type without crossing the TC4 search bounds or cutting a different log species.

steps: Harvest a log with normal loot and protection/event listeners enabled
Observe the resulting drops and axe durability
Collect the drops after they reach the player
tests.expected: Vanilla/Forge harvesting checks run once, the axe loses the normal one durability, newborn drops preserve momentum and become bubble-type Following Items with a ten-tick pickup delay.

steps: Sneak-break a log and compare with normal breaking
Use the farthest-log mode beneath leaves and listen for the bubble sound
Save/reload while converted drops are in flight
tests.expected: Sneaking performs ordinary axe harvesting, normal mode schedules nearby block updates and plays the quiet bubble cue, and Following Item state survives reload.

steps: Verify elemental tier, thaumium repair and rare rarity; compare normal and sneak use.
tests.expected: The hoe has TC4 elemental stats and sneaking keeps ordinary one-block hoe behavior.

steps: Use the hoe on mixed tillable ground with event listeners active.
tests.expected: Up to nine positions use the standard HoeItem path and respect canceled interactions.

steps: Use the hoe on vanilla and modded BonemealableBlock targets.
tests.expected: The BonemealEvent path grows valid targets and costs one durability.

steps: Test Greatwood and Silverwood saplings around 20/150 remaining durability thresholds.
tests.expected: Growth only occurs at the original thresholds and costs 5/25 durability.

steps: Observe till/growth particles and wand sound on integrated and dedicated server.
tests.expected: Effects occur once and growth/durability remain server authoritative.

steps: Exercise two clients, protection cancellation and save/reload.
tests.expected: No duplicated growth, durability or state desynchronization occurs.

steps: Verify tier/repair and compare ordinary sneak use with sounding.
tests.expected: The pick has TC4 stats and sneak bypasses all special behavior.

steps: Attack mobs and players with PVP both enabled and disabled.
tests.expected: Non-player targets ignite for two seconds and player ignition obeys the server PVP setting.

steps: Mine all mapped ores repeatedly at Fortune 0-3.
tests.expected: Fresh drops convert by the TC4 chance formula, preserving count and NBT.

steps: Sound a block near water, lava and ores at radius boundaries.
tests.expected: The client overlay lasts 100 ticks with the original radius, intensity and fade curves.

steps: Use the pick on chests, doors and machines without sneaking.
tests.expected: Block interaction is denied, sounding costs five durability, plays wandfail and swings the used hand.

steps: Test two clients, save/reload and forge ore tags for tin/silver/lead.
tests.expected: Overlay remains local and all supported tagged ores convert without stale queued drops.

steps: Verify tier/repair and compare normal with sneak mining/placement.
tests.expected: The shovel has TC4 stats and sneaking performs standard one-block behavior.

steps: Mine shovel blocks from all six clicked faces.
tests.expected: The authoritative server destroys the correct 3x3 plane and only newborn drops home to the player.

steps: Press G repeatedly, switch hands and save/reload the tool.
tests.expected: Orientations 0/1/2 cycle server-side and persist in item NBT.

steps: Copy directional/property states and test grass with dirt fallback.
tests.expected: Exact survivable states are placed, one matching item is consumed per block, and grass falls back to dirt.

steps: Test protected targets, lava, containers and blocks without item forms.
tests.expected: Canceled snapshots roll back without cost, and unsafe sources/targets are rejected.

steps: Sneak-preview with two clients and perform long placement/mining sessions.
tests.expected: Only the local player sees nine boxes and durability/drop state remains synchronized.

steps: Verify elemental tier, thaumium repair, rarity and ordinary primary damage.
tests.expected: The sword retains normal modern sword attack flow and TC4 elemental material values.

steps: Strike clustered mobs near players, owned tameables and owned golems with PVP enabled and disabled.
tests.expected: Living secondary enemies in the TC4 area are attacked; owned allies are excluded and player targets obey the server PVP setting.

steps: Hold use among mobs, items and incoming projectiles.
tests.expected: Non-player entities inside 2.5 blocks receive the original normalized outward impulse.

steps: Hold use while falling and while rising.
tests.expected: Downward velocity/fall distance are softened, lift is added and excessive upward velocity resets to 0.2.

steps: Hold use beyond several 20-tick intervals until near break.
tests.expected: One durability is spent every 20 ticks and wind/smoke effects occur without duplication.

steps: Exercise dedicated server combat, event cancellation, two clients and save/reload.
tests.expected: Secondary attacks use standard player combat events and item state remains synchronized.

steps: Verify 500 durability, tier 5, speed 8, damage bonus 4, enchantability 20, epic rarity, pickaxe/shovel actions, primal-charm repair and equipped warp.
tests.expected: The crusher exposes the original PRIMALVOID statistics, repairs with a Primal Charm and contributes two points of gear warp.

steps: Mine mixed pickaxe/shovel blocks from all six faces, then repeat while sneaking.
tests.expected: Normal mining destroys the authoritative 3x3 face plane; sneaking preserves one-block vanilla behavior.

steps: Test unbreakable blocks, claim/protection cancellation and a nearly broken crusher.
tests.expected: Every secondary block uses ServerPlayerGameMode destruction, canceled/unbreakable blocks survive and durability stays synchronized.

steps: Mine supported vanilla and tagged mod ores at Fortune 0-3 near pre-existing item entities.
tests.expected: Only fresh drops use the shared TC4 cluster chance formula; old drops are untouched and count/NBT are preserved.

steps: Damage the crusher in hotbar and inventory, observe 20-tick repair, then save/reload.
tests.expected: One durability repairs every 20 server ticks without client duplication and the damage value persists correctly.

steps: Test two clients, combat attributes, modded mineable tags and automation/tool-action queries.
tests.expected: Combat and mining remain server-authoritative while Forge consumers recognize both pickaxe and shovel digging actions.

steps: Inspect the registered item, armor points, durability, rarity, model texture and runic augmentation tooltip.
tests.expected: The item is rare, has 350 durability and one armor point, uses the original bootstraveler armor texture and accepts TC4 runic hardening.

steps: Walk forward on level dry ground with and without the boots, including sprinting and diagonal input.
tests.expected: Forward movement receives the TC4 0.055 acceleration without affecting creative flight or creating permanent attribute modifiers.

steps: Walk forward while submerged and compare dry-ground and water acceleration.
tests.expected: The ground acceleration is divided by four in water and does not run in lava.

steps: Walk into one-block ledges, repeat while sneaking, then unequip the boots during movement.
tests.expected: Normal forward movement steps up one block; sneaking disables the bonus and unequipping immediately removes only the Thaumcraft modifier.

steps: Jump while moving forward, steer in air, and fall from controlled heights with and without the boots.
tests.expected: Jump velocity gains 0.275, airborne forward control approximates the original 0.05 factor, and fall distance decreases by 0.25 each tick.

steps: Test two clients on a dedicated server, cross chunk boundaries, take armor damage, save and reload.
tests.expected: Movement remains synchronized and server-authoritative; durability, runic NBT and equipment state persist without duplicated impulses.

steps: Inspect registry type, chest armor values, durability, rarity, repair ingredient, vis discounts, runic hardening and original item/model assets.
tests.expected: The harness is a 400-durability epic chest item with three armor points, enchantability 25, gold repair, 2% general/5% Aer discount and zero-base runic augmentation.

steps: Open the harness from main hand and offhand, insert/remove filled and empty jars, shift-click, close, move the harness and save/reload.
tests.expected: The original one-slot GUI accepts only essentia jars containing Potentia, locks the source stack and serializes the exact jar stack under the jar NBT key.

steps: Wear the harness, press H with and without Potentia, enter/leave flight and remove the harness while flying.
tests.expected: H uses a server packet, enables mayfly only with fuel, plays hhon/hhoff and revokes only harness-granted flight when disabled or unequipped.

steps: Measure one Potentia unit with and without the girdle, consume the final unit, then save/reload charge and hover state.
tests.expected: Fuel follows the TC4 short charge counter: 360 threshold normally, 288 with the girdle; final depletion disables hover and all NBT persists.

steps: Fly with Haste 0-3 and the girdle, observe horizontal damping, fall distance, periodic jacobs sound and inactive harness fall softening.
tests.expected: Horizontal damping is min(1, 0.7 + 0.075*Haste + 0.21*girdle), active flight resets fall distance, jacobs repeats, and the original inactive 0.75 fall-distance factor remains.

steps: Test Curios/Baubles belt slots and the compatibility inventory mirror on two clients, then repeat in creative and spectator modes.
tests.expected: The girdle reduces positive fall distance by 0.33 per tick, modifies harness control/fuel exactly once, and creative/spectator flight is never revoked.

steps: Equip the Harness on default and slim player skins and inspect the torso from every side while standing, walking and crouching.
tests.expected: Only the original 8x12x4 torso shell is rendered, inflated by 0.6 pixels and textured from hoverharness.png without phantom arm or leg armor.

steps: Compare the worn backpack against the original hoverharness.obj and hoverharness2.png in front, rear and side captures.
tests.expected: All 124 source triangles, UVs and normals render in the original scale/rotation/translation and remain attached to the animated torso.

steps: Toggle hover on and record the two energized rings over at least sixteen client ticks.
tests.expected: The 16-frame lightningring texture animates full-bright as a 2.5 white ring and a reversed 1.5 violet ring, separated by 0.03 depth.

steps: Observe an active Harness from multiple camera angles while stationary and moving.
tests.expected: Short deterministic jagged blue-white arcs originate at the back assembly, refresh every three ticks and disappear immediately when hover is disabled.

steps: Crouch, swim, crawl, use Elytra poses where allowed, reload resources and change dimensions while wearing the Harness.
tests.expected: The shell, OBJ and effects follow the parent body transform without duplicated crouch rotation, stale model references or resource-reload corruption.

steps: Use two clients on a dedicated server and toggle each Harness independently while entering/leaving tracking range.
tests.expected: Every client sees the correct model and active effects for each tracked player; visual state follows synchronized chest-stack NBT without cross-player leakage.

steps: Place every candle colour on a valid support, inspect body/wick geometry, walk through it, remove support and observe smoke/flame.
tests.expected: All variants use the 4x8x4 wax body and 0.05x2x0.05 wick, have no collision, emit smoke/flame at y+0.7 and drop when support is removed.

steps: Place candles at a coordinate matrix including negative positions and compare drip count/side/location/height against Java Random(x + y*z).
tests.expected: Each world candle has exactly 1-5 drips and every cuboid matches TC4 side, location and 1-3 pixel height formulas.

steps: Record candles, save/reload, unload/reload chunks and restart the dedicated server.
tests.expected: A candle at the same coordinates retains exactly the same drip geometry without stored random NBT.

steps: Place and inspect all sixteen registry variants in world, GUI, hand, ground and item frame.
tests.expected: Every variant selects its matching original wax texture and the shared candlestub texture without missing sprites.

steps: Inspect all candle items in GUI, first/third person, ground and item frame.
tests.expected: Item rendering is fully 3D but contains only wax body and wick; coordinate-seeded world drips never appear.

steps: Use coloured candles around an infusion altar, reload resources and compare two clients on a dedicated server.
tests.expected: All colours remain valid stabilizers and render identical deterministic geometry for every observing client after reload.

steps: Drop stackable, unstackable and NBT-bearing items into an empty, partially filled and completely full Hungry Chest.
tests.expected: The server inserts through the exposed Forge item handler, discards only fully consumed item entities, leaves the exact partial remainder in place and does nothing when no item can move.

steps: Record repeated successful and rejected item collisions while the chest is closed and while its menu is open.
tests.expected: Every successful insertion plays one 0.25-volume eat sound with TC4 triangular pitch, sends block event (2,2) and nudges a closed lid to 0.2; rejected items are silent and do not animate.

steps: Open the 27-slot chest with one and two players, close normally, disconnect while open and wait at least 80 ticks.
tests.expected: The standard three-row menu remains valid within eight blocks, opener count is synchronized, stale viewers are corrected after 80 ticks and the lid does not remain permanently open.

steps: Place the chest facing all four horizontal directions and capture complete open/close animations.
tests.expected: The lid advances by 0.1 per tick with cubic easing, opening sound occurs only from fully closed state, closing sound occurs when crossing below 0.5, and both use volume 0.5 with pitch 0.9-1.0.

steps: Insert and extract from every side with hoppers or another Forge item handler, inspect comparator strength, then break the chest with mixed NBT stacks.
tests.expected: All 27 slots are exposed on every side, comparator output follows vanilla container fullness, and breaking drops every stored stack without duplication or NBT loss.

steps: Inspect world, GUI, hand, ground and item-frame rendering; save/reload, unload/reload the chunk and reload resources.
tests.expected: The 14x14 inset chest, exact vanilla ModelChest UV layout, original chesthungry texture and facing remain stable; inventory contents persist and the BEWLR/BER recover after resource reload.

steps: Place Arcane Bellows against floor, ceiling and all four wall faces, then walk and collide around each orientation.
tests.expected: The nozzle points toward the clicked support block in all six cases, the animated model uses the matching original transform and the axis-aware selection/collision shape remains inside the block.

steps: Observe several complete client animation cycles with no redstone power and record interpolation and sound timing.
tests.expected: Initial inflation is randomly 0.35-0.90, contraction advances by 0.075 per tick, expansion by 0.025, and one very quiet ghast-shoot sound with TC4 triangular pitch plays at full inflation.

steps: Power and unpower each Bellows orientation during contraction, expansion and while attached to supported machines.
tests.expected: Neighbor power freezes the visual state and disables every machine/furnace contribution; removing power resumes animation and functionality without duplicating ticks or sounds.

steps: Attach Bellows directly to a normal vanilla furnace, compare smelting time unpowered, powered and after save/reload, then replace the target with smoker and blast furnace.
tests.expected: Only the normal furnace detected at placement receives exactly +1 CookTime every two server ticks while actively cooking and unpowered; smoker/blast furnace are not boosted and state persists without item loss.

steps: Aim multiple Bellows from all valid sides at an Alchemical Furnace and buffer tube, then independently power each Bellows.
tests.expected: Only correctly aimed, unpowered Bellows contribute to furnace speed and tube suction; powered or misdirected units contribute zero and counts recover after chunk reload.

steps: Inspect the Bellows in world, GUI, first/third person, ground and item frame on two clients, reload resources and unload/reload the chunk.
tests.expected: The original five-part 128x64/64x32 model and byte-exact texture remain stable, client animation is smooth for both observers, and BER/BEWLR recover after resource reload.

steps: Place one Arcane Levitator in an unobstructed shaft and drop a player, mob and item through its 10-block column while recording vertical velocity.
tests.expected: Every eligible entity inside the one-block-wide column gains +0.1 vertical velocity whenever motion is below 0.35, fall distance resets each tick, and entities outside the first ten clear blocks are untouched.

steps: Enter the active column while sneaking, then repeat with items, pushable mobs, horses, spectators and non-pushable entities.
tests.expected: Sneaking stops upward acceleration and damps only negative vertical motion by 0.9; items, pushable entities and horses are affected, while spectators and ineligible entities are ignored.

steps: Build stacks of one, two and three Levitators, then power selected lower units and measure the effective column of each upper unit.
tests.expected: Each contiguous lower Levitator adds exactly ten blocks only while that lower block position is not indirectly powered; power sampled only from the block above the current unit disables the current unit but does not retroactively change the original lower-stack scan rule.

steps: Insert full solid blocks at several heights above single and stacked Levitators and repeat near the world build limit.
tests.expected: The lift column stops immediately before the first solid-render block, never extends past build height, and resumes to the correct range after the obstruction is removed.

steps: Apply and remove redstone power to the Levitator itself and to the block directly above while entities are moving in the column.
tests.expected: Either power source disables motion and particles immediately; removing power restores the same stack range without duplicated acceleration or stale state.

steps: Inspect world/item rendering and animated glow on two clients, reload resources, save and reload the world, and unload/reload the chunk.
tests.expected: The exact top, bottom, side and animated-glow resources remain stable; active glow uses legacy brightness 180, powered glow uses normal light, and counter/range/update/power caches are recomputed after reload instead of being persisted.

steps: Fill the Arcane Spa with at least 1000 mB of vanilla water, insert Bath Salts, keep mix mode enabled and leave the block unpowered.
Wait through several independent 40-tick cycles.
tests.expected: The spa consumes exactly 1000 mB and one Bath Salts only after placing one purifying-fluid source above or into the valid 5x5 expansion layer.

steps: Disable mix mode, fill the tank with a world-placeable fluid and provide a sturdy 5x5 floor around the output layer.
Let the first source form above the spa, then allow further cycles.
tests.expected: The stored fluid is placed directly above first and then expands to the first valid replaceable position in the 5x5 layer without consuming resources on failed placement.

steps: Prepare valid candidates whose only connection to the target source is above, below or on one horizontal side.
Run a dispense cycle for each arrangement.
tests.expected: All six neighboring directions count as source adjacency, matching the original BlockUtils.isBlockTouching contract.

steps: Place two spas at different times, power and unpower one of them, and observe their dispense cycles.
tests.expected: Each spa keeps its own non-persistent 40-tick counter; redstone blocks output without synchronizing every spa to world time.

steps: Insert and drain fluid plus Bath Salts through automation on side and bottom faces, then repeat from the top face.
tests.expected: Fluid and item capabilities work from non-top sides and are unavailable from above; the salt slot accepts only Bath Salts.

steps: Drop Bath Salts into a source water block and wait 200 ticks, then enter the resulting purifying-fluid source with a warped player.
tests.expected: The source water converts to purifying fluid on expiry; entering the source grants the TC4-scaled Warp Ward effect, consumes the source and plays the preserved feedback.

steps: Place the Arcane Ear on ordinary, stone, sand, glass and wood supports, then right-click through all pitches.
tests.expected: The selected note cycles 0..24 and wraps to 0; supports select harp, bass drum, snare, hi-hat and bass respectively, with volume 3 and pitch 2^((note-12)/12).

steps: Tune several ears to matching and nonmatching notes/instruments, then play note blocks at distances 63, 64 and 65 blocks.
tests.expected: Only loaded ears with both matching note and matching original five-instrument tone react at squared distance <=4096; modern extra instruments are ignored.

steps: Connect repeaters, dust and blocks on every side and below a tuned ear, then play repeated matching notes.
tests.expected: A matching note produces strength 15 weak/direct power for ten server ticks, refreshes the timer on repeated notes, then updates the ear and block below when power ends.

steps: Cover the space directly above the ear, play its configured note remotely, and right-click it.
tests.expected: Remote detection still produces the ten-tick redstone pulse, but both manual and remote note particles/sounds are suppressed while the block above is not air.

steps: Observe the ear from two clients while triggering manual and remote notes, including resource reload.
tests.expected: The model switches between original off/on side and top textures; bell geometry remains unchanged; manual notes play sound plus note particle and remote notes show only the particle.

steps: Set pitch and support tone, trigger a pulse, save/reload, unload/reload the chunk and remove the ear before playing nearby notes.
tests.expected: Pitch, tone and remaining pulse recover without stuck power, loaded-ear registration has no duplicates, and removed/unloaded ears never react to later events.

steps: Place the plate as player A, cycle all three settings and test player A, player B, mobs, dropped items and arrows.
tests.expected: Mode 0 triggers on every eligible entity; mode 1 ignores owner/authorized players but triggers on other entities; mode 2 triggers only on owner/authorized players.

steps: Measure the unpressed/pressed outline, attach dust/repeaters below and around the plate, stand on it, then leave.
tests.expected: The plate changes from 1/16 to 1/32 block height, outputs strength 15 using pressure-plate direct/weak rules, polls every 20 ticks and releases with the original click pitches.

steps: Attempt to break the plate as owner, non-owner, creative operator, explosion and piston.
tests.expected: Non-owners cannot break it, owner/creative can, entity explosions do not destroy it and pistons cannot move it.

steps: As the owner bind iron and gold keys from single stacks, larger stacks, full inventory and creative mode.
tests.expected: One blank key becomes or creates one bound key without losing unrelated stack items; full inventory drops the bound copy; creative keeps the blank and receives a bound copy.

steps: Give bound iron and gold keys to other players, try the correct plate, another plate and matching coordinates in another dimension.
tests.expected: Iron grants trigger access, gold grants trigger plus configuration/key-copy access, wrong plate/dimension is rejected and consumed keys disappear only after successful authorization.

steps: Configure the plate, authorize players, save/reload and observe from two clients while cycling and triggering it.
tests.expected: Owner UUID, both access sets and setting survive NBT; texture/settings synchronize and no client can change protected state locally.

steps: Place the Arcane Lamp on floor, ceiling and all four walls, inspect the body/nozzle orientation, then remove each support block.
tests.expected: All six placements point the Bore-textured nozzle toward the supporting face, keep the fixed world-space 8x12x8 body, emit light level 15, accept every non-air support and drop only when that support becomes air.

steps: Run one lamp in a large dark room for several minutes and record generated marker coordinates.
tests.expected: The server makes one attempt per tick using x/y/z differences of two random 0..15 values, places only in air with local raw brightness below 9 and never exceeds 15 blocks on any axis.

steps: Test lamps underground, near sea level, on mountains and near dimension build limits.
tests.expected: Candidate Y is capped at world-surface height plus 4 and raised to legacy Y=5 (or the dimension minimum); values beyond the upper build limit cancel the attempt rather than being clamped down.

steps: Allow markers to form, save/reload, unload/reload chunks, replace one with another block and remove the source lamp without invoking its normal break cleanup.
tests.expected: Markers are blockAiry-meta-3 equivalents: true air/replaceable, invisible, light level 15, no BlockEntity/source NBT/ticker, persistent until replaced or removed by an Arcane Lamp cleanup cube.

steps: Build two overlapping Arcane Lamps, let both seed lights, then break one lamp.
tests.expected: Breaking a lamp removes every Arcane Lamp light marker in the original 31x31x31 cube, including overlapping markers, while markers outside that cube remain.

steps: Observe wall/floor/ceiling lamps from two clients, reload resources and walk through marker locations.
tests.expected: The fixed body uses original lamp_side/lamp_top animation while the separate TESR nozzle uses textures/models/Bore.png; all six orientations synchronize, and markers expose only light with no outline/collision/occlusion.

steps: Eat each of the four meat nuggets at partial hunger.
tests.expected: Each restores 1 hunger, uses 0.3 saturation modifier and completes in 10 ticks.

steps: Use iron, copper, tin, silver, lead, quicksilver, thaumium and void nuggets in tag-based recipes.
tests.expected: Every TC4 nugget resolves through its forge:nuggets/<material> tag and existing TC4 recipes keep their exact registry IDs.

steps: Right-click all six primal shard aliases and the balanced shard, then test tag recipes.
tests.expected: Primal aliases preserve their aspect resonance, balanced preserves all-primal behavior, and shard/primal tags accept the aliases.

steps: Smelt all seven ore clusters in furnace and recipe viewers.
tests.expected: Each cluster is a concrete cluster item; available counted-smelting recipes yield the configured doubled output and tags identify its material.

steps: Use legacy thaumium/void ingots, amber, quicksilver, tallow and enchanted fabric in tag recipes.
tests.expected: Legacy IDs remain stable functional items and resolve through the new compatibility tags without automatic conversion to another ID.

steps: Move the 33 materialized resources through inventories, item entities, containers and save/reload with two clients.
tests.expected: Counts, names, tags and food state persist; no stack is silently migrated to a duplicate canonical ID.

steps: Inspect all six materialized passive components in creative inventory and commands.
tests.expected: Each legacy ID resolves to its dedicated item class, has the original texture and corrected EN/RU name, and is no longer a TC4ResearchComponentItem.

steps: Craft or inspect recipes using Gold Coin, Salis Mundus, Vis Filter, Mirrored Glass and Void Seed.
tests.expected: Existing normal, arcane and infusion recipes continue accepting the exact legacy registry IDs.

steps: Query the new thaumcraft item tags with commands or a recipe viewer.
tests.expected: All five crafting components and the blank golem upgrade appear in their family tags and in passive_crafting_components.

steps: Stack blank upgrades, inspect rarity/tooltip, and use them as upgrade recipe ingredients.
tests.expected: The blank upgrade stacks to 64, is uncommon, shows the translated base-component tooltip and remains distinct from installed elemental upgrades.

steps: Move the six items through player inventories, containers and item entities, then save and reload.
tests.expected: Registry IDs, counts, rarity and tags persist without migration into unrelated rebuild items.

steps: Exchange and craft with the six items between two clients on a dedicated server.
tests.expected: No client-only class loads server-side and all item properties are identical for both players.

steps: Eat Zombie Brain at low hunger repeatedly.
tests.expected: It restores 4 hunger with 0.2 saturation modifier and has an 80% chance to apply Hunger for 30 seconds.

steps: Consume many Zombie Brains while recording permanent and sticky warp.
tests.expected: Each brain grants 1 sticky warp with 10% probability; otherwise it grants 1–3 permanent warp, server-authoritatively.

steps: Eat Triple Meat Treat both hungry and full.
tests.expected: It is always edible, restores 6 hunger with 0.8 saturation modifier, and is treated as meat.

steps: Consume repeated Triple Meat Treats while injured.
tests.expected: Each use has a 66% chance to grant Regeneration I for 5 seconds.

steps: Record primal aspect pools, consume a Knowledge Fragment, and compare all six values.
tests.expected: The item is consumed and adds independently randomized 1–2 points to every primal aspect, then syncs the client pool.

steps: Use tc4_label on jars, filtered jars, tubes and alembics; craft/clear an aspect-coded label.
tests.expected: The legacy registry ID uses the same NBT aspect contract and interaction path as the canonical Jar Label, including recipe acceptance and save/reload.

steps: Inspect all 26 legacy wand caps, wand rods and staff cores in creative inventory and commands.
tests.expected: Every legacy ID resolves to TC4WandComponentItem, retains its original model/texture, and shows the corrected original EN/RU name.

steps: Assemble wands with iron, gold, thaumium, copper, silver and void caps in the Arcane Workbench.
tests.expected: All six active caps are accepted by WandComponentData and preserve their original global and aspect-specific vis cost modifiers.

steps: Attempt to assemble a wand using each inert cap before infusion.
tests.expected: Inert silver, thaumium and void caps remain visible crafting precursors but are not accepted as active wand caps.

steps: Assemble each of the eight wand rods and drain vis below ten percent.
tests.expected: Capacity matches WandRodType and elemental rods regenerate only their original matching primal aspect.

steps: Assemble each staff core, including Staff Core of the Primal, then inspect capacity and regeneration.
tests.expected: Staff capacities are 125/175/250 as defined by TC4; the primal core regenerates all six primal aspects.

steps: Exchange all component families between two clients, assemble wands, save and reload the world.
tests.expected: Registry IDs, component recognition, assembled rod/cap NBT and tooltips remain stable on a dedicated server.

steps: Fill an empty tc4_essence phial from an alembic and jar, then pour it back into both containers.
tests.expected: Exactly 8 essentia moves per operation, the same registry item becomes filled/empty through NBT, and no essentia or phial is duplicated.

steps: Craft a labeled jar using every filled tc4_essence aspect and inspect the two-layer item tint.
tests.expected: The dynamic label recipe accepts the legacy phial, returns the same empty carrier, and the overlay uses the stored aspect color.

steps: Open common, uncommon and rare legacy loot bags repeatedly.
tests.expected: Each stack is capped at 16, rarity is common/uncommon/rare, and every opening emits 8–12 generated loot stacks with the original coin sound.

steps: Right-click Crimson Rites before and after CRIMSON research is unlocked.
tests.expected: The book is not consumed, CRIMSON progression and its warp/attunement side effects are applied server-side, and repeated use does not duplicate the research key.

steps: Use the exact tc4_eldritch_object ID on a valid Eldritch Altar and also right-click it in air.
tests.expected: The altar accepts and consumes the eye when valid; in-air attunement follows the existing Eldritch progression gate exactly once.

steps: Use tc4_eldritch_object_2 on an inactive Eldritch Lock, then save/reload and repeat on an active lock.
tests.expected: Only the runed tablet activates and is consumed by an inactive lock; lock state persists and no duplicate activation occurs.

steps: Give tc4_thaumonomicon and tc4_thaumonomiconcheat, open each on a client connected to a dedicated server.
tests.expected: The normal legacy book opens the player research view; the cheat book opens the completed/creative view without a duplicate menu or client-class server crash.

steps: Create tc4_researchnotes and tc4_discovery stacks with research NBT, use them in the Research Table, save, quit and reload.
tests.expected: Both exact legacy IDs are recognized as ResearchNoteItem instances and preserve their research key, hex data and completion state.

steps: Open tc4_focuspouch and tc4_focuspouchbauble, move several wand foci, close, transfer the pouch to another player and reload the world.
tests.expected: Both aliases open the focus-pouch menu and preserve the same per-stack inventory without duplication or item loss.

steps: Exercise tc4_gogglesrevealing, tc4_inkwell and tc4_soap through their normal revealing, scribing and warp-cleansing paths.
tests.expected: Each legacy ID follows the canonical concrete item behavior, consumes durability/items only on the server and synchronizes its result to the client.

steps: Set permanent, sticky and temporary warp to distinct values, hold tc4_sanitychecker in the main hand, test totals below/equal/above 100 and toggle Hide GUI.
tests.expected: The original 20x76 frame appears at the upper-left, the 48-pixel gauge shows the three violet segments in temporary/sticky/permanent order, the cap marker appears at 100+, and Hide GUI suppresses the overlay.

steps: Equip every tc4_focus_* legacy ID on a compatible wand and exercise its normal cast path on a dedicated server.
tests.expected: Excavation, Fire, Frost, Portable Hole, Primal, Shock, Equal Trade and Warding use the canonical WandFocusItem runtime; the blank and orphaned reversal sprites remain explicit passive components.

steps: Equip every runic ring, amulet and girdle legacy ID through Curios and through the fallback inventory adapter, then take damage and trigger charged/regeneration/kinetic/emergency effects.
tests.expected: Curios classifies the items through vanilla item tags, runic charge is consumed by the existing shield runtime, and no hard Curios dependency is required on a server without Curios.

steps: Store primal vis in lesser and normal tc4_vis_amulet stacks, equip one, hold a partially empty wand and observe several five-tick transfer cycles.
tests.expected: The lesser amulet caps each primal at 25 vis, the normal amulet at 250 vis, and at most 0.05 vis per primal moves into the held wand every five server ticks without duplication.

steps: Create several untagged tc4_mana_bean stacks, wait for aspect attunement, eat them while full and sample potion/aspect-pool outcomes.
tests.expected: Each stack receives one stable TC4 aspect, remains always edible meat with 1 nutrition and 0.5 saturation, grants one random potion outcome, and has the original 25% chance to add one point of its aspect.

steps: Equip, dye, damage and reload every cloth, cultist robe/plate/leader and void robe legacy ID; inspect vis discount, runic charge, warp tooltip and textures.
tests.expected: Armor uses the reconstructed TC4 material values and textures; cloth/void dyes and repair ingredients work, robe discounts apply, cultist robes expose 1 warp, Void Robes expose 2 warp, the Void hood reveals nodes/popups, and Void pieces repair one durability per second while worn.

steps: Use tc4_nitor as the existing Nitor item, fill and empty tc4_phial against supported essentia containers, and use tc4_charm in preserved recipes.
tests.expected: The exact legacy IDs route through the canonical Nitor and 8-unit essentia phial behavior while the primal charm remains a concrete crafting component rather than a generic fallback.

steps: Place tc4_arcanedoor, test both halves as owner and stranger, bind iron/gold Arcane Keys, authorize another player, then operate it from an adjacent powered Arcane Pressure Plate with matching and nonmatching access lists.
tests.expected: The two halves share one UUID owner/access record, only authorized users may toggle or break it, keys normalize to the lower half, ordinary redstone is ignored, and only an adjacent powered warded plate sharing an authorized UUID opens it.

steps: Damage tc4_crimson_blade, carry it for several seconds, strike living targets, repair it with tc4_charm, and inspect its attributes, tooltip and warp contribution.
tests.expected: The blade has 200 durability, tier 4 / speed 8 / +3.5 tier damage / enchantability 20, repairs one durability every 20 ticks, applies Wither for 60 ticks and Weakness for 120 ticks, accepts the primal charm for repair, and contributes 2 warp.

steps: Use tc4_ironbell on owned and unowned golems, golem markers and compatible golem blocks, then save and reload.
tests.expected: The exact legacy ID executes the canonical GolemBellItem selection, pickup and binding behavior without a generic-item fallback or duplicated state.

steps: Use tc4_resonator on every face of filled and empty jars, void jars, reservoirs, alembics and essentia tubes with typed and untyped suction.
tests.expected: The resonator reports stored essentia/buffer entries and the clicked-face suction amount/type from supported endpoints, reports empty state cleanly, and plays the original alembic-knock sound server-authoritatively.

steps: Use tc4_ob_placer as survival and creative players on a top face, test blocked targets at every actual placement offset, then inspect the resulting vertical structure.
tests.expected: Only creative players may use the migration utility; it validates every block it actually replaces and creates the Eldritch altar/stone/obelisk column at offsets +1 and +3 through +7 without consuming the item.

steps: Carry tc4_sinister_stone near DARK and non-DARK nodes at several distances, place walls between the player and node, and observe the model while moving across chunk boundaries.
tests.expected: The normal stone switches to the active sprite only for a visible DARK node within 256 blocks and returns inactive when line of sight/range/type no longer matches.

steps: Load inventories or migration commands containing tc4_lightningring and tc4_sinister_stone_active, inspect their models/tooltips and verify creative-tab exposure.
tests.expected: Both old sprite-derived IDs remain loadable compatibility aliases, are not presented as invented standalone TC4 mechanics, and the active stone alias always renders the active sprite.

steps: Place all eight Greatwood/Silverwood legacy BlockItems, grow both legacy saplings with bonemeal, break logs/leaves/planks and reload the world.
tests.expected: Every tc4_block_* tree alias places the functional modern tree block; saplings generate the matching tree family and saves reload without missing registry entries.

steps: Place Shimmerleaf, Cinderpearl and Vishroom on sturdy blocks, inspect light/particles, then walk a survival mob and player through Vishroom.
tests.expected: The flowers retain their luminous ambient presentation; Vishroom applies ten seconds of Confusion to living entities and all three aliases drop/load under their exact legacy IDs.

steps: Surround an Ethereal Bloom with each supported taint soil/fibre/crust/flesh/flux block inside and outside eight blocks, keep chunks loaded for several minutes and reload.
tests.expected: The bloom conservatively removes explicit rebuild taint blocks within the TC4-sized purification radius without mutating biome data or touching targets outside the radius.

steps: Walk and sprint across tc4_block_paving_travel as a player and another living entity while observing effects and expiry after leaving.
tests.expected: Entities on the stone continually receive Speed II and Jump Boost I for 40 ticks, with effects expiring shortly after leaving.

steps: Drop airborne non-player living entities over tc4_block_paving_warding, repeat while the stone is redstone-powered, and verify player behavior.
tests.expected: An unpowered stone pushes airborne non-player living entities away; redstone disables the ward and players are never pushed.

steps: Place and break the Arcane Stone slab, Metal Base and all six Obsidian Totem variants in survival; inspect models, slab halves/double state, tool requirements and drops.
tests.expected: Every exact legacy block has a valid blockstate/model, pickaxe tags and self-drop loot; a double slab drops two items.

steps: Load and place the old IDs for advanced furnace, alchemical construct, alembic, pedestal, bellows, essentia storage/transport, infusion matrix, mnemonic matrix and node devices.
tests.expected: Each legacy BlockItem places the already-portable canonical 1.19.2 functional block, preserving old inventory IDs without creating duplicate world-state implementations.

steps: Attach tc4_block_lamp_growth to an essentia tube carrying Herba, surround it with immature BonemealableBlock crops, and observe at least 100 successful growth operations plus the reserve cycle.
tests.expected: The lamp advertises 128 Herba suction on its mounted input face, consumes one essentia into 100 charges, keeps one reserve essentia, and spends one charge per successful server-side growth action within radius 6.

steps: Attach tc4_block_lamp_fertility to a Victus tube; test two adult same-species animals, an over-populated group of eight, babies, and animals already in love.
tests.expected: The lamp stores at most four Victus charges, every 300 ticks puts one eligible adult pair in love for two charges, and skips babies, existing love mode and species populations above seven.

steps: Insert a partially empty wand and then a Vis amulet into tc4_block_wand_pedestal, place unjarred primal aura nodes within eight blocks, and inspect charging, item capability and comparator output.
tests.expected: Only a wand or Vis amulet occupies the single exposed item-handler slot; one whole vis (100 centivis) is transferred every five ticks from an eligible primal node point and comparator strength tracks total fill.

steps: Place tc4_block_wand_pedestal_focus above a recharge pedestal, use the topper to insert/remove a target, and provide a nearby node containing only compound aspects.
tests.expected: The topper survives only above its base and delegates interaction; it consumes one eligible compound node point and recursively converts it into one whole vis (100 centivis) of a component primal that has room.

steps: Place a partially empty wand or Vis amulet on tc4_block_wand_pedestal beside an unjarred aura node containing a known aspect, observe several successful five-tick charge pulses from multiple camera angles, then remove the target item or exhaust all eligible node aspects.
tests.expected: The stored item bobs with sin(t/16)*0.05 and rotates one degree per tick; each successful transfer renders the original textures/misc/wispy.png floaty line from pedestal y=1.65-2*bob to the drained node, tinted with the drained primal or compound aspect color, and the line stops after charging stops.

steps: Build the exact 3x4x3 Node in a Jar structure around a live aura node: three complete glass layers with the node replacing the centre of the middle layer and nine wooden slabs on top; activate glass, slab and node positions with a charged wand, then repeat with one invalid material and with insufficient primal vis.
tests.expected: Only clicks whose TC4 origin scan reaches a valid structure are handled; the complete ritual atomically consumes the cap-discounted equivalent of 70 centivis of Aer, Terra, Ignis, Aqua, Ordo and Perditio, removes 26 glass plus nine slabs, and replaces the live node with one filled Node in a Jar block. Invalid material or insufficient vis leaves the structure and node intact.

steps: Capture controlled NORMAL, BRIGHT, PALE and FADING nodes with known current/base aspect amounts and stable NodeId values; repeat enough seeded captures to exercise both modifier branches.
tests.expected: Current aspects, base aspects, type and NodeId are copied exactly with no percentage loss. Each capture independently has a 75 percent chance to weaken the modifier by one step (BRIGHT to NORMAL, NORMAL to PALE, PALE to FADING, FADING unchanged) and otherwise preserves it.

steps: Break a freshly captured jar, inspect the dropped stack, place it elsewhere, save/reload the world, then use a wand on the placed jar and try charging a wand pedestal from the released node.
tests.expected: The filled stack and placed BlockEntity preserve NodeId, type, modifier, current/base aspects and scan metadata through break, inventory, placement and reload. Wand release replaces the jar with a live unjarred aura node, produces one glass-break event, drops no duplicate jar, and the released node is immediately eligible for normal tapping and pedestal recharge.

steps: Observe ritual completion from first- and third-person views, then inspect the filled item in GUI, hand and ground transforms and the placed jar after relogging.
tests.expected: The world renderer reuses the original jar shell and animated node layers; on capture the complete jar scales from 3.0 to 1.0 over one second using 1 + 2 * remainingSeconds, remains visible outside the unit block bounds during the effect, and the item renderer displays the same stored node profile without loading client classes on a dedicated server.

steps: Enter the Magical Forest with differently attuned Mana Beans and use each bean on the underside of oak, spruce, Greatwood and Silverwood logs; repeat outside a magical biome and against other faces/blocks.
tests.expected: A bean places exactly one age-0 Mana Pod in the air block below a supported log only when the clicked face is DOWN and the biome belongs to thaumcraft:is_magical. The planted pod preserves the bean aspect and consumes one bean outside creative mode; invalid placements change nothing.

steps: Plant controlled adjacent Mana Pods with known aspects, advance random ticks until stage three, and repeat with one, two and several distinct neighbour aspects using seeded worlds.
tests.expected: Pods expose ages 0 through 7, attempt one growth pass with probability 1/30 per random tick, and choose their aspect at age 3. Distinct source aspects each enter the pool once; every valid ordered combination result enters twice, matching TC4 weighted crossbreeding. Empty ancestry yields HERBA with probability 1/8 and otherwise a random primal aspect.

steps: Break pods at every age while preserving their BlockEntity aspect, including repeated mature age-7 harvests and support-log removal.
tests.expected: Ages 0 and 1 drop nothing. Ages 2 through 6 drop one Mana Bean carrying the pod aspect. Age 7 drops one bean or, when the random roll is greater than 0.33, two beans with the same aspect. Support loss follows the same drop path rather than deleting the stored aspect.

steps: Generate fresh Magical Forest chunks with deterministic seeds and inspect pod positions, support blocks, ages and aspects; generate equivalent non-magical chunks as controls.
tests.expected: Each fresh Magical Forest chunk performs ten Mana Pod attempts beginning at Y=64. Successful pods hang below valid logs, begin from legacy age 2..6 and immediately receive one growth pass, yielding ages 3..7 with a valid TC4 aspect. No Mana Pods are generated by this pass outside magical biomes and no adjacent chunk is force-loaded.

steps: Observe all eight ages in first- and third-person views with several primal and compound aspects, including reload and multiplayer chunk tracking.
tests.expected: Ages 0, 1 and 2 use the original stem_0, stem_1 and stem_2 textures; ages above 2 render the original pulsing inner pod, while the outer pod blends from TC4 green toward the stored aspect colour and reaches the exact aspect colour at age 7. The block light value equals its age and aspect NBT remains synchronized after reload.

steps: Scan the same attuned Mana Pod with a Thaumometer at ages 6 and 7, then harvest/replant it with a different aspect and repeat after save/reload.
tests.expected: Immature pods do not expose a dynamic container aspect. At age 7 the scan target and authoritative completion path expose exactly one point of the stored pod aspect, matching TC4 TileManaPod IAspectContainer behavior; the result follows the replanted aspect after reload.

steps: Open a v11.62.x/v11.63.x test world containing legacy mapped stacks in chests, Thaumcraft machines, an Arcane Pedestal and a Thaumatorium; load each chunk and restart the server.
tests.expected: The deferred migration converts only allow-listed duplicate IDs, preserves count/damage/NBT/capabilities, marks the chunk schema ledger once and does not rescan it after restart.

steps: Load players, mobs, container minecarts and dropped items carrying allow-listed legacy stacks in main inventory, armor/offhand and Ender Chest slots.
tests.expected: All supported live storage surfaces migrate server-side without item loss, duplication or client-only class loading.

steps: Place allow-listed legacy stacks inside nested portable inventories or focus pouches up to four levels deep and load/save/reload them.
tests.expected: Writable Forge item handlers are migrated recursively while unsupported read-only wrappers remain untouched and never crash world loading.

steps: Load a region with many old chunks while profiling chunk tickets and server tick time.
tests.expected: Chunk callbacks only enqueue work, at most two currently loaded chunks are inspected per level tick, getChunkNow never force-loads neighbours and unloaded queued chunks are skipped safely.

steps: Run schema 116343, inspect the SavedData counters, then test a fixture with an older or missing schema value.
tests.expected: Matching schema data retains processed chunks and counters; older schemas intentionally start a fresh bounded pass and are persisted with setDirty after changes.

steps: Run the required Forge GameTest essentiaJarPersistsFilterAndContents on the headless GameTest server.
tests.expected: A filtered jar accepts only its aspect and preserves filter, aspect and amount through a real BlockEntity NBT round-trip.

steps: Run the required Forge GameTest wandPedestalDrainsNodeAndChargesWand.
tests.expected: Five server ticks rescan the nearby live node, remove exactly one Aer point, add exactly 100 centivis to the wand and record the visual drain source.

steps: Run the required Forge GameTest nodeJarPersistsProfileAndCaptureAnimation.
tests.expected: The filled jar starts its collapse at scale 3 and preserves NodeId plus current aspects through BlockEntity serialization.

steps: Run the required Forge GameTest manaPodGrowsAndPersistsAspect.
tests.expected: A supported age-2 pod grows to age 3, keeps a single-parent aspect, exposes nothing before maturity, exposes one aspect at age 7 and preserves it through NBT.

steps: Run the required Forge GameTest legacyStackMigrationPreservesPayload.
tests.expected: An allow-listed legacy stack becomes the canonical item without losing count, custom name or custom NBT.

steps: Run the required Forge GameTest arcaneDoorUpperHalfDelegatesPersistentAccess.
tests.expected: The upper door half delegates owner, authorized-user set and key binding position to the persistent lower half.

steps: Energize normal, bright, pale, fading and unstable nodes with known compound aspect profiles; drain the same primal several times within one server tick and again on the next tick.
tests.expected: The source reduces its base profile recursively to primals, applies the TC4 modifier multiplier, floors the square root and exposes only that per-tick centivis pool. Multiple consumers share one tick's pool, the next server tick restores visBase, and no permanent node aspect is removed or invented.

steps: Build relay chains with links at 7.9 and 8.1 blocks, insert/remove solid walls, and cycle neighbouring relays through wildcard and all six attunements with a wand.
tests.expected: Only loaded relays or energized nodes within eight blocks and direct line of sight form graph edges. Wildcard -1 joins any colour; otherwise both relays must match. Wand use cycles -1,0..5, refreshes the parent path and survives save/reload.

steps: Wear lesser and normal Vis Amulets near a connected relay, both with and without a wand in the main hand, and repeat just below/above squared distance 26 from the relay.
tests.expected: Every five ticks the amulet first gives at most 5 cv per primal to the held wand, then independently requests at most 5 cv per primal from the nearest relay. Recharge occurs only while the relay centre is at true squared distance below 26 and capacity is available.

steps: Place a Vis Charge Relay above an Arcane Workbench with a partially empty wand and connect it through one and several relays to an energized node.
tests.expected: The charger requests at most 5 centivis per primal per server tick, not 500 cv or whole node points. All requests share the energized source's transient tick pool and pulse the exact relay path.

steps: Observe idle and consuming relay chains with and without Revealing gear, from multiple camera angles and after chunk reload.
tests.expected: Every relay renders beam1.png to its persisted parent as two crossed additive quads of half-width 0.105. Idle opacity is faint, Revealing gear restores full visibility, a consume pulse raises opacity for five ticks and colours each relay in the drained primal colour before fading toward white.

steps: Save/reload a multi-relay network, unload/reload its chunks, remove an intermediate relay and change attunement while another player watches.
tests.expected: Attunement, parent position and pulse data synchronize through BlockEntity update tags/packets. Parent links refresh every 40 server ticks or immediately after wand tuning, never force-load chunks, and stale paths disappear after topology changes.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.energizedNodeUsesTransientPerTickPool on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: A 25-Aer energized node exposes five centivis in one server tick, refills to five on the next tick, and never changes its permanent aspect profile.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.visRelayGraphHonorsLosAttunementAndPersistence on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: A blocked direct edge routes through a visible matching Aer relay; changing the neighbour to Ignis breaks the path, while attunement and the parent position survive NBT round-trip.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.workbenchChargerTransfersFiveCentivisPerTick on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: A Vis Charge Relay above an Arcane Workbench transfers exactly five Aer centivis in one server tick from an energized node and leaves permanent node aspects unchanged.

steps: Place an energized Aer node/relay network and a Flux Scrubber with flux goo and gas inside radius 16; observe several complete shuffled scan cycles.
tests.expected: The scrubber requests up to 10 Aer centivis while below five power, spends exactly five per removed flux block, examines at most 16 shuffled positions per tick, never force-loads chunks, and ignores flux at squared distance 256 or greater.

steps: Let the scrubber accumulate cleanup charges, attach an essentia tube to its mounted/facing side and pull the recovered essentia into a labelled jar.
tests.expected: Every four cleanup charges are consumed in one conversion attempt; the original 25% branch adds at most one Praecantatio, storage caps at four, and only the facing side can export without advertising suction or accepting input.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.fluxScrubberRemovesFluxAndExportsPraecantatio on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: A primed scrubber removes one nearby flux block, records one cleanup charge, rejects extraction from the wrong face, exports Praecantatio from its facing side and preserves power/charge/essentia counters through NBT round-trip.

steps: Smelt the three ore clusters, a balanced shard and a TC4 gold coin in a vanilla furnace.
tests.expected: Iron and gold clusters yield two ingots with 1.0 XP, cinnabar yields two exact tc4_quicksilver items with 1.0 XP, tc4_shard_balanced yields one tc4_dust with 1.0 XP, and tc4_coin yields one gold nugget with 0 XP; all use 200 ticks.

steps: Inspect the infusion recipe registry and Thaumonomicon/JEI pages for INFUSIONENCHANTMENT.
tests.expected: All 24 original recipes are present: Repair and Haste plus 22 vanilla enchantments, with their exact original component lists, aspect bases, instability and research gate.

steps: Infuse a compatible enchanted item repeatedly and compare XP, essentia, instability, compatibility and maximum-level rejection.
tests.expected: XP is recipeXP*(1+current target level), instability adds half the sum of existing enchantment levels, essentia scales by target level plus 10% of other levels, output increments only the target enchantment, and incompatible/max-level targets do not start.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.originalSmeltingRecipesMatchTC4 on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: The live RecipeManager exposes all five exact TC4 furnace recipes with the correct input ids, output ids/counts, XP and 200-tick duration.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionEnchantmentFamilyMatchesTC4 on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: The runtime registry contains 24 infusion enchantment recipes and Protection validates original components, XP/instability/essentia formulas, level increment, preservation of compatible enchantments and max-level rejection.

steps: Load the strict alchemy recipe registry with and without compatible tin, silver and lead content mods, then throw each tagged ore into a correctly primed crucible.
tests.expected: PureTin, PureSilver and PureLead resolve oreTin/oreSilver/oreLead through forge:ores/tin|silver|lead, consume exactly Metallum 1 + Ordo 1 and return the matching TC4 ore cluster without requiring a Thaumcraft-owned ore.

steps: Craft gold, copper, inert silver, inert thaumium and inert void caps using the original NNN/N N pattern, testing both Thaumcraft nuggets and compatible third-party nuggets in Forge tags.
tests.expected: Gold requires exact vanilla gold nuggets; copper/silver/thaumium/void accept their forge:nuggets tags. Vis costs are 3/2/4/6 and void uses Perditio 27 + Ordo 27 + Ignis 18 + Aer 18.

steps: Infuse inert silver, thaumium and void caps with two, three and four Salis Mundus components respectively.
tests.expected: Charged caps preserve TC4 instability 4/5/8 and craftCost-derived aspects: silver Potentia 8/Auram 4, thaumium 12/6, void Potentia/Vacuos/Alienis/Auram 18 each.

steps: Smelt Greatwood and Silverwood logs, Cinnabar Ore and Amber Ore in a vanilla furnace.
tests.expected: Both magical logs yield one charcoal with 0.5 XP; Cinnabar Ore yields one quicksilver_drop and Amber Ore yields one amber, each with 1.0 XP and 200-tick duration.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.oreDictionaryCrucibleAndLegacySmeltingMatchTC4 on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: The live custom alchemy registry exposes the three Forge-tag pure-metal catalysts and RecipeManager exposes all three exact legacy smelting inputs/outputs, including both magical log variants.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.wandCapRecipeFamilyMatchesTC4 on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: All five generated arcane cap recipes accept exact/tagged nuggets with original patterns and Vis formulas, while all three charged-cap infusion recipes preserve catalyst, Salis Mundus count, aspects and instability.

steps: Craft Tube Restrict, Tube Oneway and Essentia Crystallizer with legacy-equivalent Forge/vanilla tags.
tests.expected: stone, dyeBlue, ingotIron and plankWood substitutions are accepted through #forge:stone, #forge:dyes/blue, #forge:ingots/iron and #minecraft:planks without changing pattern or Vis costs.

steps: Load and execute all eight original wand-rod infusion recipes.
tests.expected: Every rod preserves its exact catalyst, ordered shard/rod components, instability and craftCost-derived aspect ledger; the ice rod uses ordinary ice.

steps: Infuse goggles and each of the three fortress faceplates into a wildcard Thaumium Fortress Helm.
tests.expected: The central helmet is retained and receives goggles byte 1 or mask int 0/1/2 after consuming the exact original components.

steps: Execute JarBrain, Mirror, MirrorHand, MirrorEssentia and TravelTrunk infusions.
tests.expected: MCP fields resolve to water bucket, stick/compass/map, iron or gold ingots, ender pearl and the original TC4 aliases rather than stale resolver guesses.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.mcpCorrectedArcaneAndWandRodRecipesMatchTC4 on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: The live bundled recipe managers expose all three corrected arcane records and the eight exact wand-rod infusions.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.legacyNbtAndMirrorInfusionsMatchTC4 on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: NBT-only helm upgrades and the corrected brain, mirror and travel-trunk component lists match the original source contract.

steps: Load and execute WardedGlass, NodeRelay, NodeChargeRelay, JarVoid, HungryChest, AlchemyFurnace, TubeFilter and Centrifuge.
tests.expected: The live recipe manager preserves every original pattern or shapeless input, flattened metadata alias, result count and Vis cost.

steps: Execute GolemFlesh in the crucible with a Flesh Block catalyst.
tests.expected: One Flesh Golem item is produced after consuming Humanus 8, Motus 8 and Spiritus 8.

steps: Infuse each of the eight flattened golem material items with shears, glowstone dust, gunpowder, an essentia jar and a zombie brain.
tests.expected: Every legacy material variant retains its central item identity and receives byte tag advanced=1 with Cognitio/Sensus/Victus 8 and instability 3.

steps: Run finalOriginalArcaneAndGolemFleshRecipesMatchTC4 and advancedGolemWildcardFamilyMatchesTC4 on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: The final ten historical records are exposed through exact runtime paths and the historical runtime-registration ledger is statically mapped at 258/258; runtime loading remains unverified.

tests.notes: Load a fixture containing removed item ids, verify MissingMappings remap, NBT/capability preservation, and second-restart idempotence.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.auraNodeLegacyItemConvertsStoredNodeNbt on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Legacy aura_node NBT converts deterministically into a Node in a Jar, preserving node aspects and the custom stack name; empty payloads are rejected.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.boneBowRetainsOriginalFastChargeContract on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Bone Bow retains 512 durability, enchantability 3, full power at 10 ticks, the original charge curve and the 18-tick model cap.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.travelingTrunkPersistsInventoryUpgradeAndCapability on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: The 27-slot trunk preserves upgrade, custom name, named stacks and Forge item-handler capability across an entity NBT round-trip.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.crimsonCultistRolesKeepAttributesAndPersistence on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Cultist, Knight, Cleric and Praetor entity types keep their role-specific attributes and the Praetor role survives save/load.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.fortressArmorSetAndMaskNbtMatchTc4 on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: The three-piece fortress set, goggles byte, mask int, fire resistance and original 1.30 masked-set multiplier are preserved.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.tallowCandleKeepsSupportShapeAndInfusionStabilizerContract on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: A supported tallow candle has no collision, creates the render-anchor BlockEntity, implements the infusion stabilizer contract and becomes invalid after support removal.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.hungryChestEatsPersistsAndExposesSingleInventory on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Hungry Chest consumes an accepted dropped stack through its single 27-slot inventory, preserves custom NBT/name, triggers the eat lid event, exposes item-handler capability and survives NBT round-trip.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.brainJarAbsorbsPersistsAndReportsComparatorExperience on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Brain in a Jar absorbs touching XP, stores the exact amount, reports the original comparator scale and preserves XP through NBT.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.magicMirrorLinksTransportsAndPersistsQueuedStacks on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Two Magic Mirrors establish a reciprocal link, transport a tagged item entity, apply count-based instability, persist the target queue and invalidate both endpoints cleanly.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.filteredAndVoidJarsKeepTc4SuctionCapacityAndOverflow on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Filtered jars enforce their aspect and 64-point capacity; filtered void jars consume overflow while retaining the original continuous suction, with filter and contents preserved through NBT.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.essentiaReservoirKeepsMixedStorageFacingAndCapacity on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: The reservoir exposes only its configured side, stores mixed essentia up to 256 points, drops suction when full and preserves facing plus aspect amounts through NBT.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.essentiaMirrorDrainsOnePointAndRollsBackRemoteJar on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: A linked Essentia Mirror accepts only one-point requests, drains the selected remote jar and restores the exact source on rollback.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.essentiaMirrorUsesOnlyForwardEightBlockSourceVolume on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: The remote mirror ignores sources behind its face, scans the original forward [0,8) volume and drains a valid reservoir at the range edge.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.normalAndRestrictedTubesPropagateOriginalJarSuction on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Normal tubes propagate adjacent jar suction as neighbour minus one, while restricted tubes halve the neighbouring suction.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.filteredTubeLocksSuctionAndTransferToLabelAspect on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: A filtered tube propagates labelled-jar suction only for its selected aspect and rejects mismatched essentia.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.oneWayTubePropagatesSuctionOnlyAlongOriginalFacing on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: A one-way tube propagates suction only through the original facing-controlled direction and clears suction when rotated away.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.bufferTubeCapsPersistsAndSynchronizesRollbackState on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: A buffer tube caps mixed essentia at eight points, keeps untyped side suction/choke state, persists NBT and synchronizes its legacy transport amount after drain/rollback.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.alchemicalFurnaceProcessesPersistsAndRespectsCapacity on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: The Alchemical Furnace consumes fuel and input on its persisted local phase, respects the original 480-aspect capacity and preserves inventory, fuel and essentia through NBT.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.furnaceFeedsOnlyFourAlembicsOnLocalFortiethTick on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: The furnace uses its own persisted counter, feeds at most four vertically stacked alembics and switches to the twenty-tick phase when bellows are present.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.alembicKeepsCapacityFilterSidesAndNbt on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: An alembic accepts compatible essentia up to 32 points, honours its filter and blocked faces, and preserves type, amount and facing through NBT.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.centrifugeOnlySplitsCompoundFromBelowWithRedstonePause on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: The centrifuge accepts one compound aspect from below, pauses under redstone, completes the original 39-step process and exposes a component only from the top.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.thaumatoriumRequiresHeatAndRedstoneStopsSuction on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: The Thaumatorium requires heat, requests the first missing aspect with suction 128 and suspends suction while powered.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.thaumatoriumCraftClearsEntireLegacyEssentiaBuffer on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: A successful Thaumatorium craft consumes one catalyst and replaces the complete internal AspectList, including unrelated residual legacy essentia.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.mnemonicMatricesExpandThaumatoriumFormulaMemoryByTwo on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: The base Thaumatorium exposes one formula and each adjacent Mnemonic Matrix adds two selectable formula slots.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.blockedThaumatoriumOutputPreservesCatalystAndEssentia on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: A blocked output preserves catalyst and paid essentia; crafting resumes atomically once output space becomes available.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionUsesOnlyOriginalStabilizerWhitelist on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Only original TC4 IInfusionStabiliser blocks and the legacy skull metadata family grant stabilizer credit; altar structure and node machinery do not.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionStabilizerScanPairsAroundMatrixBlock on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Stabilizers are mirrored around the runic matrix block, paired once, with unpaired positions retained in a deterministic signature.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionInitialInstabilityUsesSingleOriginalSymmetryPass on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Initial instability is exactly symmetry plus recipe instability, clamped to 0..25, without a second auxiliary stabilizer pass.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.runningInfusionOnlyRaisesInstabilityWhenLayoutWorsens on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: A running infusion may gain instability when surroundings worsen but does not erase accumulated risk when stabilizers are added later.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionChangedCatalystRollsExactlyOneInstabilityBranch on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: A changed locked catalyst enters the weighted instability table exactly once before cancellation.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionChangedCatalystAddsNoAutomaticFailureWarp on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Catalyst replacement does not receive an additional generic terminal Warp award after the single weighted event.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionSuccessAddsNoAutomaticWarp on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Successful infusion completion does not grant unconditional Warp.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionMissingComponentWaitsInsteadOfCancelling on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: A missing pedestal component leaves the active craft waiting and retrying rather than cancelling it.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionStructureLossPreservesLockedRecipe on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Periodic validLocation failure deactivates the matrix but preserves the locked recipe, pending essentia/components and crafting state.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionStructureLossAddsNoTerminalWarp on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Breaking the center pedestal or a required pillar pauses/deactivates the matrix without terminal failure Warp.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionReactivationResumesLockedCraft on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: After the altar structure is restored, wand reactivation resumes the same saved craft rather than selecting or clearing a recipe.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionComponentLayoutDoesNotReselectRecipeAfterStart on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Once crafting starts, removing or moving a required component does not trigger recipe reselection or terminal failure; the matrix waits for a matching component.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionSaveReloadKeepsCraftingFlag on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: A saved and reloaded matrix keeps active/crafting progress rather than becoming a fresh inactive altar.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionSaveReloadKeepsLockedRecipe on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: The recipe id, locked catalyst id and catalyst stack snapshot survive world save/reload.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionSaveReloadKeepsPendingCosts on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Pending essentia and component queues survive save/reload without being recomputed from current pedestals.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionSaveReloadKeepsTravellingComponentSource on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: A selected travelling component source pedestal and stack snapshot survive reload before final consumption.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionChunkUnloadDoesNotCancelCraft on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Chunk unload/reload preserves the running craft and does not execute a cancellation or terminal failure path.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionInstabilityEventTableRollBoundMatchesOriginal on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Weighted instability table uses the original nextInt(21) roll gated by the original nextInt(500) <= instability check.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionInstabilityEventTableGateMatchesOriginal on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: The gate only allows the weighted event roll when the gate roll is less than or equal to the current clamped instability, matching the original.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionInstabilityEventTableIsTotalAndNonOverlapping on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Every one of the 21 original roll slots maps to exactly one event outcome with no gaps or overlaps.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionInstabilityEventTableCosmeticSurgeIsDefaultBranch on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Eject-type-0, single-target harm, and single-target zap branches keep their original roll-count sizes (4, 2, and 3 respectively).

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionInstabilityEventTableExplosionAndWarpMatchOriginal on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Matrix self-explosion (roll 9, strength 1.5F + random) and warp (roll 20, 25% sticky +1 else permanent 1-5) match the original TileInfusionMatrix#craftCycle exactly.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionEssentiaAndComponentShortageNeverCancelTheCraft on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: A brief essentia or component shortage waits and retries instead of cancelling the locked infusion craft, matching the original craftCycle.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionEssentiaShortageAttemptsEveryPendingAspectPerCycle on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Essentia shortage retries every pending aspect in the same cycle and only stops once one aspect actually drains, matching the original recipeEssentia.getAspects() loop.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionEssentiaShortageForcesResurveyButComponentShortageDoesNot on the Forge GameTest server.
