tests.expected: Essentia shortage forces an immediate surroundings re-scan (checkSurroundings = true) while component shortage does not, matching the original craftCycle asymmetry.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionComponentShortageFirstUnmatchedIngredientAlwaysRefundsEssentia on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: The first unmatched ingredient index (a == 0) always refunds one essentia point, matching the original nextInt(1 + a) with a == 0.

steps: Run TC4BlockEntityGameTests.infusionComponentShortageRefundOddsShrinkPerUnmatchedIndex on the Forge GameTest server.
tests.expected: Refund odds shrink per unmatched ingredient index (nextInt(1 + a)) and essentia/component instability-creep bases match the original 100/50 formulas.

tests.description: Behavioral: lockedRecipeAspectTypes ledger populates and random aspect refund path compiles with production constants
InfusionInstabilityEvents delegates gate to TC4InfusionInstabilityEventTableParity
Every TC4 core includes AIAvoidCreeperSwell at priority 0
FISH core includes AIFish at priority 2 with mutex 3
USE core includes AIUseItem at priority 0 with mutex 3
All 8 materials match TC4 EnumGolemType: health/armor/speed/fireResist
Arcane Workbench Ordo cost = 2 centivis, vis scaled x100
6 original recipe book entries, all Ordo 2
GUI 190x234, 6 primal aspects at TC4 coordinates
3 TC4 fallback recipes: Thaumium(4 Praecantatio), Nitor(3I+3P+3L), Dust(2P+1O+1Perd)
Liquid Death: 50% per-aspect crystal drop, min 1, count = max(1,random/2)
19 wand/staff rod entries including creative adapter; exact capacities and craft costs
7 cap entries including infinity adapter; exact base and aspect-specific vis modifiers
Elemental rod 200-tick and primal-staff 50-tick regeneration below 10% capacity
Jar/reservoir capacities and normal/filtered/void suction strengths
Jar and reservoir five-tick active pull cadence
Six tube subtypes, suction transforms and 2/5/20 tick production cadence
Essentia mirror range 8 and one-point transfer requests
Six tainted replacements, slime fallback, spread offsets and spore gate
Magical Forest/Eldritch biomes, tree entry points and ten mana-pod attempts
Runic shield, thaumic damage sources and fortress armor constants
Warp thresholds, Crimson Rites and Outer Lands constants
Six node types and Node Jar modifier degradation
48 aspect labels and 18 separate smelting-bonus registrations
Golem decorations, bell modes and carry formula
Ten focus types and 21 contiguous upgrade ids
Liquid Death exact crystal count formula
Thaumometer 25-tick hold and completion at remaining count five
Ten pre-completion camera ticks with original volume and pitch interval
Handheld known-target rejection and entity-before-block target priority
RESEARCHER2 10% free-placement decision occurs before aspect pool/table bonus availability.
RESEARCHER1/2 clear refunds retain strict 25% and 50% thresholds; Mastery placement retains strict 10%.
Research Table bonus recalculation retains the original counter threshold 600 and radius 8 scan.
Real ResearchTableBlockEntity save/load collapses positive duplicate bonus amounts to one serialized point per aspect type, matching TC4 NBT.
Real ResearchTableBlockEntity consumption remains removed after save/reload while unconsumed bonus types persist.
Real ResearchTableBlockEntity serverTick preserves TC4 nextRecalc++ > 600 boundary: 600 advances to 601, 601 fires and resets to zero.
Real ResearchTable menu combines Aer + Perditio using one player-pool point and one table-bonus point as a single committed transaction.
A missing second component rejects the pair without consuming the available first component.
A resource-complete but invalid aspect pair consumes both selected points, matching TC4 GuiResearchTable/PacketAspectCombinationToServer.
A same-aspect request with only one point fails preflight and cannot partially debit that point.
A real Research Note accepts an available type-0 hex even when it does not yet touch a compatible occupied neighbour, matching TC4 placement semantics.
The authoritative server path rejects a forged placement for an undiscovered aspect without consuming ink or aspect points.
The authoritative server path rejects an occupied/anchor hex without overwriting note state or debiting resources.
The real Research Table placement debit restores both scribing-tool ink and the selected aspect source when the final note mutation is rolled back.
Research Note edit packets require a live, distance-valid server Research Table menu and cannot target a stale or fabricated container.
A real placed Research Note hex clears to type 0, consumes exactly one ink and does not refund an aspect without Expertise or Mastery.
Research Expertise refunds below 0.25 and excludes the exact 0.25 boundary while clearing a real note hex.
Research Mastery refunds below 0.50 and excludes the exact 0.50 boundary while clearing a real note hex.
Creative mode bypasses ink damage but does not invent the port-only unconditional clear refund absent from TC4.
A real clear debit can restore exact Research Note NBT, scribing-tool ink and the original aspect pool after a simulated downstream failure.
The authoritative server clear path rejects empty type-0 and locked anchor hexes without consuming ink or changing note state.
Research Note completion requires the authoritative live Research Table menu and exact note identity.
A connected known-aspect graph completes and prunes disconnected non-anchor hexes like TC4 ResearchManager.checkResearchCompletion.
An already completed Research Note rejects a second completion without changing solved NBT.
An exact-NBT completion snapshot rejects a note changed before commit and cannot mark it solved.
A completed discovery consumes one note even in creative and unlocks eligible sibling research after its target.
A mutated solved-discovery snapshot cannot unlock research or consume the note.
Research completion warp 0/1/2/3/5 follows the original permanent/sticky bucket split.
A five-point research grant produces permanent 3, sticky 2, temporary 0 and total/counter 5.
Repeating an already completed research unlock cannot grant permanent or sticky warp twice.
Infectious Vis Exhaust propagates as amplifier-1 and terminal amplifier zero downgrades to ordinary Vis Exhaust.
Propagated infectious exhaustion keeps the default milk cure, unlike the initial warp-event instance.
The initial infectious exhaustion created by a warp event remains uncurable as in TC4.
Sanity Soap consumes one item in both creative and survival, matching the original direct stack decrement.
Sanity Soap chance bonuses and purifying-fluid Warp Ward duration follow the original constants and formula.
Warp guardians and mind spiders preserve TC4 tri-state axis signs: negative, zero and positive outcomes for 7-24 block magnitudes.
Warp entity spawn candidates require a solid top surface, collision-free entity bounds and no liquid, without a fixed two-air-block gate.
A 0.3-block Mind Spider can use low clearance that rejects the 2.2-block Eldritch Guardian, matching TC4 entity-aware collision checks.

warp.text.8 is the BATHSALTS milestone line (actual warp > 10), shown once via TC4EldritchProgression using TC4WarpRuntimeParity.BATHSALTS_MILESTONE_MESSAGE_KEY. The blurred-vision warp event (effectRoll<=36) applies the effect with no chat line. Corrects the v11.64.06 misattribution.

ELDRITCHMINOR (actual warp > 25) and ELDRITCHMAJOR (actual warp > 50) silently call grantResearch(10)/grantResearch(20) via TC4EldritchProgression with no chat line, matching TC4 checkWarpEvent. Corrects the port's fabricated milestone chat literals and missing research grants.
Warp grantResearch rolls 1..times points only from the six TC4 primal aspects and synchronizes the changed aspect pool to the affected client after the batch.

While Unnatural Hunger is active, finishing rotten flesh or a zombie brain removes 600 ticks and one amplifier, removes level-zero/expired results, restores only rotten flesh as the explicit cure on a surviving effect, and ordinary food shows the TC4 warning without canceling nutrition.

Sun Scorned samples the entity/world brightness table rather than raw-light/15, preserves the strict >0.5 burn and <0.25 heal bands, sky visibility, exact random inequalities, and neutral-band RNG short-circuiting.

Death Gaze uses TC4 EntityUtils.isVisibleTo geometry: 0.75-radian full aperture, body-midpoint targeting, strict axial projection < range round cap, and pickable-entity admission without a spherical distance gate.

Unnatural Hunger matches TC4 end to end: 0.025 exhaustion per level per tick, 5000/6000 event durations, min(3, warp/15) amplifier, 600-tick/one-level special-food reduction, original effect color/icon coordinates and exact shader transform constants.

With Unnatural Hunger active, the dedicated post chain processes before the HUD using vanilla color_convolve with ColorScale [1.0,0.8,0.8] and Saturation 1.1, then blits to minecraft:main; the old green rectangle is absent.

The dedicated PostChain loads lazily, resizes with the framebuffer, survives resource reload by invalidating and recreating, closes when the effect/player disappears, and does not occupy GameRenderer global effect slot.

On a dedicated server, effect duration/amplifier and food-finish reduction synchronize to the affected client; rotten flesh/brain and ordinary-food messages use the original keys/styles without canceling nutrition.
The 18x18 mob-effect icon is a pixel-exact crop of TC4 potions.png at icon index (7,1), and the full potion sheet is byte-identical to the original JAR.
Pure contract verifies 200-tick lifespan, fluid properties, Warp Ward identity/formula, crucible aspects and original wizard price range.
Production expiry path converts only an exact vanilla water source at the item entity position and rejects flowing water.
A purifying source grants amplifier-0 Warp Ward using permanent-warp sqrt duration and consumes itself exactly once.
Purifying fluid uses the three exact TC4 particle-sheet frames 16-18 with alpha 0.25, full-bright rendering, original motion, damping and final-frame progression.
The fluid emits one bubble per animate tick and a 1/25 lava-pop using separate x/z rolls, y+1, volume 0.1..0.2 and pitch 0.9..1.05 before superclass RNG.
Bath Salts, pure bucket and fluid textures are byte-identical to the original TC4 resources; bubble sprites are exact crops from particles.png.
The pure bucket has default item rarity, stack size one, returns an empty bucket in survival, places a source and can pick up a source through Forge fluid bucket semantics.
Arcane Spa accepts Bath Salts, consumes one salt plus 1000 mB vanilla water on its 40-tick cycle and emits purifying source fluid through the original 5x5 expansion contract.
Hidden BATHSALTS remains at (-4,-4), complexity 2, four research aspects at 3, exact 6-each crucible recipe, and parent of SANESOAP and ARCANESPA.
Dedicated server owns source consumption and MobEffectInstance creation; amplifier, duration, ambient/visible flags and block removal synchronize to clients.

Original wizard trade contract is fixed at 5+nextInt(3) emeralds for one Bath Salts. Runtime activation remains tied to the separate custom wizard-villager system and must not be substituted with a vanilla profession.
Production registrations and pure parity contract verify capacity, interaction delay, release bound, comparator scale, item stack, light and enchanting bonus.
A touching XP orb may overflow 2000 during absorption exactly like TC4; the next tick start clamps the stored value to 2000.
Right-click sets the exact 40-tick eating delay and break release preserves the complete remaining XP total using vanilla orb splitting.
Below capacity and outside eat delay, the nearest XP orb in the original 6-block inflated jar box receives the exact /7 quadratic pull with 0.15 horizontal and 0.33 vertical multipliers.
Each shake uses nextInt(min(xp+1,64)), subtracts the selected amount, splits it into vanilla XP orbs at block centre, and never releases 64 in one shake.
Only XP is persisted and synchronized; transient eatDelay resets on reload, including migration from earlier port saves that contained EatDelay.
Comparator output is zero when empty and floor(xp/2000*14)+1 when non-empty, reaching 15 at capacity.
Brain in a Jar contributes exactly 2.0 enchanting power when accepted by the modern enchanting-table placement rules.
World and item renderers use the exact TC4 ModelBrain 128x64 boxes/pivots, ModelJar.Brine 64x32 box, original textures, bob, scale and rotations.
At 2000 XP the jar emits the original position/color/alpha spell-particle contract through a dedicated translucent particle type.
The brain tracks the nearest XP orb or player within six blocks, idles by 0.01 radians, lerps by 0.04, and uses the original real-time brain-sound schedule and pitch range.
Hidden JARBRAIN at (-5,9), complexity 2, exact research aspects, INFUSION parent, entity/item triggers, research warp 3, item warp 1 and exact infusion recipe remain wired.
XP absorption, shake release, break release and comparator updates are server-owned and synchronize the XP-only block-entity update tag to clients.
brain2.png, jarbrine.png and jar side/top/bottom textures are byte-identical to original TC4 assets; block model uses the exact two original render cuboids.
Registered Hungry Chest verifies the exact 27-slot inventory, 64 stack limit, 14/16 outline, 15/16 collision, drop bounds and cubic lid easing.
Only dropped items intersecting the original inset collision body are accepted; compatible stacks fill first, partial remainder survives, and full rejection is unchanged.
Open/close block events, ten 0.1 lid steps, 0.2 eat nudge and TC4 tile-identity-only menu validity are exercised on the production block entity.
Breaking a chest splits each occupied slot into original 10..30 item chunks at one per-slot position while preserving total count, name and NBT.
Forge item automation, comparator values 1 and 15, Items-list persistence and transient lid/open-state exclusion share the real production inventory.
Entity ingestion uses the collision body x/z 1..15 pixels and y 0..15 pixels, while selection remains 14 pixels high.
Insertion merges strict-compatible stacks before empty slots, plays one 0.25-volume triangular-pitch eat sound only after movement and sends block event (2,2).
Each slot samples one 0.1..0.9 position and repeatedly emits min(remaining,nextInt(21)+10) chunks with 0.05 Gaussian motion and +0.2 vertical bias.
World renderer uses vanilla 1.7 ModelChest boxes/UVs and byte-exact chesthungry.png with original cardinal metadata rotations.
Client lid follows 1-(1-angle)^3; server open sound occurs only from zero and close sound when crossing 0.5 downward at original volume/pitch.
BEWLR uses the single original BlockChestHungryRenderer inventory transform: Y rotation 90 degrees then translation -0.5 on all axes.
Exactly 27 slots persist through the vanilla Items list; opener count and lid interpolation remain transient across save/reload.
The original unsided inventory is exposed from every Forge capability side and comparator output is computed from the same 27 slots.
Secondary HUNGRYCHEST at (-1,0), complexity 1, Fames/Vacuos 3, exact two pages, child GOLEMSTRAW and arcane WTW recipe with Aer5 Ordo3 Perditio3 remain wired.
Inventory mutation, break drops, comparator changes and eat decisions are server-owned; opener and eat lid state synchronize through original block-event IDs 1 and 2.
All 16 registry entries preserve the exact TC4 metadata order, Utils.colors tint values and Arcane Pech offer bound 0..14.
A placed candle uses the TC4 6/16..10/16 by 8/16 selection shape, no collision and light level 14.
A candle survives only on a solid top support and removes itself when that lower support is lost.
Coordinate-seeded wax drips preserve the original Java Random sequence and all 16 colours remain infusion stabilizers.
TALLOW remains in ALCHEMY at (-2,0), complexity 1, Corpus 3/Praecantatio 1, parent CRUCIBLE and exact three pages.
Each of the 16 candle colours places as its own modern registry block, retains its colour when broken and drops exactly itself.
Candles place only on blocks with a solid upper face; wall and unsupported placement are rejected exactly as TC4 BlockCandle.
All colours use the single original animated candle.png and exact Utils.colors runtime tint while candlestub.png remains white.
Body, wick and 1..5 coordinate-seeded drip cuboids use original dimensions and RenderBlocks-style cropped UVs.
Smoke and flame spawn at block center +0.7Y and the block emits vanilla integer light 14 from original setLightLevel(0.95).
BEWLR renders body plus white wick with the original -0.5 inventory translation and no world drips or approximate context transforms.
TALLOW exposes the exact 3-candle string/tallow recipe, 15 dye recolours, whitewash wildcard and Lux2 Corpus1 Praecantatio1 object aspects.
Every colour is accepted by the infusion stabilizer scanner with identical stabilizing behavior.
JEI shows the base recipe, all 15 recolours, whitewash and every registered candle item without duplicate legacy metadata items.
Placement, support loss and drops are server-owned while exact colour, animation, particles and drip geometry are client-derived deterministically.
The original Arcane Pech reward contract contains candle metadata 0..14 at weight/tier 1 and excludes black metadata 15; activation awaits full original Pech trade parity.
Arcane Bellows retain the fixed 0.1..0.9 full-height box, six-direction orientation and original lowercase orientation/onVanillaFurnace NBT only.
Initial random inflation, asymmetric 0.075/0.025 stroke, top overshoot sound and inventory sine animation match TC4.
Horizontal furnace acceleration works every two ticks while the original vertical X/Z-only lookup quirk remains.
Alchemical Furnace accepts six oriented unpowered bellows while Crucible counts four horizontal bellows regardless of facing.
BELLOWS research and exact Infernal Furnace cook-time/bonus constants remain source-grounded.
Bellows place on all six clicked faces with nozzle orientation opposite the clicked side.
Neighbor redstone freezes the client stroke and disables generic machine contribution exactly as TileBellows.
Vanilla furnace CookTime advances only for 1..198 and never at 0 or 199.
Crucible heat gain remains 1 plus two per horizontal bellows with its original orientation/redstone exception.
Alchemical Furnace scans all six directions and applies 12.5 percent smelt-time reduction per active bellows.
Essentia buffer suction is bellows times 32, with choke 1 forcing one and choke 2 forcing zero.
World renderer uses original ModelBellows cuboids, frame/bag UV sizes, exact orientation transforms and un-clamped inflation.
BEWLR uses one original inventory transform and player-tick sine inflation without approximate per-context transforms.
BELLOWS remains ARTIFICE (-6,-2), parent INFERNALFURNACE, Aer6 Machina3 Motus3, with exact WW / LCI / WW Arcane recipe and Aer10 Ordo5.

Infernal Furnace external contract retains five distance-two scan directions, cap three, cook times 140/80 minus 20 each and 44 percent bonus chance per bellows; activation awaits full Infernal Furnace parity.
Arcane Ear cycles notes 0..24, uses the exact exponential pitch formula and a ten-tick redstone pulse.
Support materials map to the five TC4 tones and a solid block above suppresses local sound/particle.
Exact note/tone matches within distance squared 4096 emit a transient strength-15 redstone pulse and only one buffered event per tick.
Only lowercase note and tone persist; pulse and powered render state never revive after reload.
ARCANEEAR research, Sensus 4 object aspects, common rarity, stack size and full collision remain exact.
Right-click advances one of 25 notes and plays the configured tone only when the space above is air.
Played note-block events are matched by tone and note across a 64-block radius in the same dimension.
A match powers all sides at strength 15 for ten ticks and notifies the ear and block below on start/end.
World save/reload preserves note and support-derived tone while clearing signal timer and powered model state.
World and item models use the exact ten cuboids and seven byte-identical original Arcane Ear textures.
Powered state switches only side/top textures; bell textures stay fixed and note sound/particle use exact volume, pitch and height.
ARCANEEAR remains ARTIFICE (6,0), parent GOGGLES, Sensus3 Potentia3 Aer3, with exact GIG/GBG/WRW recipe and Aer10 Ordo10.
Scanning the Arcane Ear returns exactly Sensus 4 as registered by ConfigAspects.
JEI shows greatwood planks, redstone, iron, gold and zombie brain in the exact Arcane Ear pattern without stale shears mapping.
Note detection and redstone are server-owned while block events synchronize powered textures, sound and particles to clients.

steps: Run the dependency-free and Forge GameTest checks for range, exact vertical thresholds, sneak descent and the item/pushable/horse admission predicate.
tests.expected: All exact floating-point boundaries and admission cases match TileLifter.

steps: Build a stacked pair, clip the upper column with an opaque block and save/reload its block entity.
tests.expected: The lower unit adds ten blocks, the first opaque block clips the range, and transient counter/range/update/power fields are absent from NBT.

steps: Spawn an item in an active production lift column and execute the real block-entity ticker.
tests.expected: Y motion gains the original increment, X/Z are unchanged and fall distance resets to zero.

steps: Check block support faces, redstone connection faces, hardness, rarity and stack size.
tests.expected: Only horizontal faces support/connect, hardness is 2.5 and the ordinary item stacks to 64.

steps: Resolve LEVITATOR from the research bridge and Levitator from the recipe bridge.
tests.expected: Coordinates, parent, flags, research aspects, pattern and vis costs match TC4.

steps: Power a lower stack position and separately power only the block above another lower unit.
tests.expected: Stack contribution follows the original lower-position power query rather than the current-unit power helper.

steps: Power the levitator position, then remove it and power the block immediately above.
tests.expected: Either source disables entity lift immediately and updates the rendered powered state.

steps: Save while active with a nonzero range and reload the world and chunk.
tests.expected: Counter, range, requiresUpdate and lastPowerState are not stored; the fresh block entity recomputes from world state.

steps: Compare active and powered block models under controlled block/sky lighting.
tests.expected: Active glow uses legacy brightness 180 converted to block light 11; powered glow uses normal ambient light.

steps: Inspect the world model and the inventory-only model from every face.
tests.expected: World rendering uses exposed top/side planes with colors 0x00A000/0xDD11FF; inventory uses the two original inset volumes with 0x00A000/0xEECCFF.

steps: Observe the custom sparkle at all particle settings and inspect its four retained atlas frames.
tests.expected: The type-3 green color, size, gravity, damping, lifetime and sprite progression match FXSparkle; no vanilla enchant substitute appears.

steps: Open the LEVITATOR research after completing NITOR.
tests.expected: The concealed ARTIFICE entry appears at -3,-3 with Motus 3, Volatus 3, Aer 3 and text plus Arcane-crafting pages.

steps: Inspect the Arcane Workbench recipe in JEI and craft it.
tests.expected: Pattern WEW/BNB/WAW maps Greatwood, Terra shard, iron, Nitor and Aer shard with Aer 10 and Terra 5.

steps: Reload resources while active and while powered.
tests.expected: Animated glow, custom particle sprites, tint colors and active/powered lighting survive reload without missing models.

steps: With two clients in the same column, let one player sneak while the other does not.
tests.expected: The modern server-safe adaptation applies sneak descent only to the sneaking entity and keeps authoritative server motion synchronized.

steps: Run arcanePressurePlateProductionTriggerModesMatchOriginal.
tests.expected: The real block entity reproduces modes 0/1/2 for owner, authorized players and non-player entities.

steps: Run arcanePressurePlateFloatingBoundsSignalsAndWardsMatchOriginal.
tests.expected: The plate floats, has exact 1/16 and 1/32 outlines, empty collision, original redstone outputs and warded hardness.

steps: Run arcanePressurePlateLegacyOwnerAccessNbtRoundTrip.
tests.expected: Legacy owner/access/setting tags survive load and save with 0/1 access prefixes.

steps: Run arcaneKeyOriginalLocationTypeAndMigrationContract.
tests.expected: Keys write original location/type NBT and early rebuild Bound/X/Y/Z keys still match.

steps: Run arcanePressurePlateOwnerWandRemovalDropsOnePlate.
tests.expected: The owner removes an unpressed plate with a wand and receives exactly one plate item.

steps: Run arcanePressurePlateKeysResearchAndRecipeIndexMatchOriginal.
tests.expected: WARDEDARCANA, plate recipe, iron/gold keys, rarity and stack sizes match the source index.

steps: Place mobs, dropped items and players on setting 0.
tests.expected: Every entity that does not ignore block triggers powers the plate.

steps: Authorize one player, set mode 1, compare owner/authorized/outsider/mob.
tests.expected: Owner and authorized players are excluded; outsiders and non-player entities trigger it.

steps: Authorize one player, set mode 2, compare owner/authorized/outsider/mob.
tests.expected: Only owner and authorized players trigger the plate.

steps: Bind the last blank iron and gold keys in survival with a full and non-full inventory.
tests.expected: A new bound copy is inserted or dropped before one blank key is consumed; the hand stack is not replaced.

steps: Try copying keys as owner, full-access user and ordinary user.
tests.expected: Owner copies iron/gold; full-access user copies iron only; ordinary user copies neither.

steps: Save and restart with owner, standard and full access plus setting 2.
tests.expected: UUID and original name-based owner/access data and setting remain authoritative after restart.

steps: Inspect all three settings pressed/unpressed and bound/blank key tooltips.
tests.expected: Exact TC4 textures and heights render; blank keys have no extra tooltip; bound keys show three purple italic lines in x,z,y order.

steps: Inspect and craft ArcanePressurePlate, IronKey and GoldKey.
tests.expected: Plate uses T=Thaumium and D=Greatwood; gold recipe outputs two gold keys; all WARDEDARCANA pages resolve.

steps: On a dedicated server bind and consume keys with two clients, reconnect and retest modes.
tests.expected: Server owns inventory consumption, access grants, redstone state and persistence without client desync.

steps: Run arcaneLampParityMathMatchesOriginalBounds.
tests.expected: Triangular offsets, surface/minimum clamp and Arcane Bore phase table match TC4.

steps: Run arcaneLampAcceptsAnyNonAirSupportAndDropsOnAir.
tests.expected: A non-sturdy non-air support is accepted; air support is rejected.

steps: Run arcaneLampLightMarkerIsAirAndHasNoBlockEntity.
tests.expected: Marker is air, invisible/non-colliding, light 15 and has no BlockEntity.

steps: Run arcaneLampOrientationNbtRoundTripsOriginalOrdinal.
tests.expected: The orientation integer saves and restores the six-direction state.

steps: Run arcaneLampCleanupUsesOriginalSharedRadiusCube.
tests.expected: All shared markers at Chebyshev radius 15 are removed; radius 16 remains.

steps: Run arcaneBoreAdjacentLampSeedsOriginalTunnelMarker.
tests.expected: An adjacent ordinary Arcane Lamp enables the original post-mining tunnel marker production path.

steps: Run arcaneLampResearchRecipeAndRarityMatchOriginal.
tests.expected: Research index, S/IAI/N recipe components and common item rarity match TC4.

steps: Place the ordinary lamp on all six faces and inspect the separate nozzle after resource reload.
tests.expected: Only the Bore.png nozzle rotates; the fixed lamp body never rotates its top/side texture assignment.

steps: Mount an Arcane Lamp directly to an Arcane Bore Base and observe both sides.
tests.expected: A second opposing Bore.png connector nozzle renders on the bore-base support exactly as TC4 TileArcaneLampRenderer.

steps: Mine repeatedly with an adjacent lamp and record d=2*nextInt(32), lateral and vertical offsets.
tests.expected: Distances are even 0..62; phases apply +3,0,-3,0 lateral and phase 3 lowers horizontal tunnels by two.

steps: Save six oriented lamps, restart dedicated server and reload chunks.
tests.expected: NBT orientation ordinals 0..5 restore exact attachment direction and renderer orientation.

steps: Open ARCANELAMP recipe in Thaumonomicon and JEI.
tests.expected: Pattern is space-S-space / IAI / space-N-space with glowstone, iron, arcane stone and nitor; vis costs are 8 Ignis, 8 Aer, 4 Aqua, 4 Perditio.

steps: Run the Arcane Spa parity constants/candidate-order GameTest.
tests.expected: Capacity 5000, bucket 1000, immediate then 40-tick cadence, and x-outer/z-inner offsets -2..2 match TC4.

steps: Use a full water bucket on a spa containing 4500 mB.
tests.expected: Only 500 mB enters, the whole filled bucket is consumed, one empty bucket is returned, and no GUI opens.

steps: Use an empty bucket on a spa containing fluid.
tests.expected: No fluid drains and the Arcane Spa GUI opens.

steps: Save with TC4 keys and load an early-port Mix/Tank/Salts payload.
tests.expected: New saves use lowercase mix, root FluidStack and root Items; legacy port saves migrate without loss.

steps: Mix water and Bath Salts, then perform the next expansion cycle.
tests.expected: The first source appears directly above; later expansion follows x=-2..2 outer and z=-2..2 inner order with six-direction adjacency.

steps: Probe capabilities from every side and power/unpower the spa.
tests.expected: UP exposes neither fluid nor item automation; all other/unknown sides do, and redstone pauses cycles without consuming resources.

steps: Switch to fill mode and validate the registered recipe/research/block properties.
tests.expected: World-placeable stored fluid is emitted without salts; ARCANESPA and ArcaneSpa recipe metadata match TC4; the block is stone/common/non-opaque.

steps: Use a filled fluid container while the spa is full, then while it contains another fluid.
tests.expected: The interaction is consumed but the container is not spent and the GUI does not open, matching BlockStoneDevice metadata 12.

steps: Cancel or force failure of the world block placement after a valid output target is chosen.
tests.expected: The spa has already consumed 1000 mB and, in mix mode, one Bath Salts before the placement attempt, matching TC4 order.

steps: Run fill mode with vanilla water in an ultra-warm dimension and compare with lava or another placeable fluid.
tests.expected: Vanilla water is not placed; other world-placeable fluids follow their normal output path.

steps: Save a mixed-mode spa with fluid and salts, restart the dedicated server and reload the chunk.
tests.expected: mix, root FluidName/Amount and root Items restore mode, exact fluid amount and slot stack.

steps: Load worlds saved by v11.63.24-v11.64.22 containing Mix, Tank and Salts compounds.
tests.expected: Mode, fluid and salt inventory migrate once into the exact TC4-compatible representation.

steps: Connect item/fluid pipes to UP, DOWN and four horizontal faces.
tests.expected: UP cannot insert/extract or fill/drain; every other side can expose the corresponding handler.

steps: Open the GUI at several tank levels and inspect the world/item model after resource reload.
tests.expected: Six full 8x8 fluid tiles are drawn then masked from the top; gauge/frame and spa top/side/pedestal-bottom textures align exactly.

steps: Open the same spa from two clients, toggle mix mode and move Bath Salts while fluid changes through automation.
tests.expected: Mode, slot, fluid amount/type and block updates remain authoritative and synchronized without client-only mutation.

steps: Open ARCANESPA in the Thaumonomicon and inspect the JEI arcane recipe.
tests.expected: Pattern QIQ/SJS/SPS uses piston, essentia jar, Arcane Stone, quartz blocks and iron bars with 16 Aqua, 8 Ordo and 4 Terra.

steps: Run arcaneBoreParityFormulasMatchOriginal.
tests.expected: Width, delay, near-broken boundary and Perditio acceleration credits match TC4.

steps: Run arcaneBoreSpiralUsesOriginalRotatedLaneContract.
tests.expected: Two-degree TCVec spiral selects distinct rotated integer lanes.

steps: Run arcaneBoreRequiresVerticalBaseAndExtendsTowardNozzle.
tests.expected: Bore requires a vertical base and its shape extends one block toward the nozzle.

steps: Run arcaneBoreSavesOriginalInventoryListAndOrientations.
tests.expected: Inventory list, Slot bytes, orientation, baseOrientation and SpeedyTime use original NBT.

steps: Run arcaneBoreBaseRetainsAllFacePerditioSuction.
tests.expected: All faces connect; nozzle suction is zero and other faces request Perditio at 128.

steps: Run arcaneBoreMenuAndItemsRetainOriginalContracts.
tests.expected: Bore/Base rarity and menu/NBT contracts match TC4.

steps: Run arcaneBoreResearchAndRecipesMatchOriginal.
tests.expected: ARCANEBORE links exact base and infusion recipes.

steps: Power the bore head, then only its base, with valid tools installed.
tests.expected: Either signal runs the bore; no signal pauses target work without disabling repair.

steps: Provide Perditio aura vis and a Perditio tube to the base.
tests.expected: Bore drains up to 100 centivis / 5 first, then one essentia for +20 while below 20 speed credit.

steps: Install a damaged repair-enchanted pickaxe, remove redstone, and provide required vis.
tests.expected: Machine repair and special pickaxe inventoryTick continue every 40/5 ticks while unpowered.

steps: Mine supported ores with elemental pickaxe or Dowsing focus upgrades.
tests.expected: Native cluster chance is 0.2 + fortune*0.075 with original ore mappings.

steps: Point the base nozzle at an inventory, fill it, then mine another block.
tests.expected: Drops insert through the nozzle side first; remainder ejects at 0.075 horizontal and 0.025 upward velocity.

steps: Save/reload bore and base with tools, orientations and SpeedyTime; repeat with old Forge Inventory compound and SpiralIndex.
tests.expected: Original NBT reloads, legacy inventory migrates, and SpiralIndex is intentionally reset/not re-saved.

steps: Attach tubes to every base face and rotate nozzle with a wand.
tests.expected: Every face is connectable, output face has zero suction, and other faces draw Perditio at 128.

steps: Observe active/idle bore, rotate it in six directions, inspect GUI with near-broken pickaxe and upgrades.
tests.expected: Separated original model pieces, dual beam, three vortex layers, jar core, impact particles and exact half-scale GUI render correctly.

steps: Run two clients on dedicated server, rotate bore/base, mine, unload/reload chunk and reconnect.
tests.expected: Server-authoritative target, inventory, drops, NBT and menu sync remain consistent without client classes loading server-side.

steps: Run thaumometerAspectPoolCapsMatchTc4.
tests.expected: Rewards remain raw below 100, sqrt at 100 and one at 125.

steps: Run thaumometerDiscoveryBonusPrecedesCaps.
tests.expected: The +2 discovery bonus is included before pool caps.

steps: Run thaumometerNodeVisibilityConstantsMatchTc4.
tests.expected: Held-main-hand node reveal uses 48 range and 0.44 cone.

steps: Run thaumometerReadoutLayoutMatchesTc4.
tests.expected: Readout uses 5/4/3/2/1 rows, max 15 and original node text colour.

steps: Run thaumometerTitleShrinkFormulaMatchesTc4.
tests.expected: Names shrink by 0.000025 per pixel after width 90.

steps: Run thaumometerStableNodeIdentityMatchesTc4.
tests.expected: Node ledger uses NODE plus persistent node UUID.

steps: Run thaumometerItemContractMatchesTc4.
tests.expected: Item is single-stack, uncommon, 25-tick and 10-block range.

steps: Start a scan, copy/swap the Thaumometer, look away, release early and reconnect.
tests.expected: Pending scan is never saved to ItemStack NBT and every target/hand/item change cancels it.

steps: Scan blocks whose picked item differs by variant and scan a damageable item entity.
tests.expected: Ledger identity follows picked item/meta rules and damageable items use meta -1.

steps: Scan adult/baby mobs, player names, villager zombie, swelling/powered creeper and multiple golem materials.
tests.expected: Each original ScanManager suffix creates the expected stable identity.

steps: Scan an object matching several hidden/lost research triggers.
tests.expected: Exactly one random @RESEARCH clue is granted; full research and warp are not granted.

steps: Attempt to scan a compound aspect before and after discovering both parents.
tests.expected: Scan fails with the missing-parent notification until both parents are known.

steps: Load a scanner containing ScannedBlocks, ScannedEntities, ScannedAuraNodes and pending tags from an older port.
tests.expected: Knowledge migrates once to player data and all rebuild-only scanner/pending tags are removed.

steps: Scan a node, jar/move or reload it while preserving NodeId, then inspect through a new scanner.
tests.expected: The node stays known by NODE+UUID and coordinate-only keys migrate.

steps: Inspect unknown/known blocks, entities and nodes in first person and view nodes through walls.
tests.expected: Unknown target shows title only; known aspects/type render on original glass; only main-hand scanner reveals nodes.

steps: Two clients scan the same and different targets, change hands mid-scan, die/reconnect and use fresh scanners.
tests.expected: Server owns each player ledger and transient scan; no client-only classes load and knowledge synchronizes independently.

steps: Register two TC4ThaumometerPhenomenaRegistry handlers, leave the normal ray empty, scan the first returned phenomenon, then unregister it and scan the second.
tests.expected: Handlers are queried only after entity/node/block targeting, first non-null wins, stable phenomenon knowledge persists and synchronizes through the dedicated phenomena ledger.

steps: Open a normal book after completing a research whose sibling completion is absent.
tests.expected: The missing original sibling is completed with original side effects and research/aspect data synchronize.

steps: Use the Cheat Sheet with mixed known and unknown aspects/research.
tests.expected: Only original research entries complete; newly discovered aspects receive exactly 50 pool and existing pools are not overwritten.

steps: Right-click a bookshelf using an unfocused wand.
tests.expected: Bookshelf is removed, one zero-velocity special Thaumonomicon spawns with enchant particles and wand sound, with no vis cost.

steps: Right-click a bookshelf with a wand that has any focus installed.
tests.expected: No transformation, item spawn, particles or vis consumption occurs.

steps: Purchase an eligible secondary research with exact and insufficient aspect pools.
tests.expected: Server silently consumes exact aspects only when affordable and grants eligible siblings; insufficient purchase changes nothing.

steps: Start primary research with and without paper, inked scribing tools or an existing note.
tests.expected: Packet is sent only with paper, ink and no open note; server consumes inputs and creates one note without fabricated chat.

steps: Inspect/use the in-book compound recipe for wand plus bookshelf.
tests.expected: Recipe is 1x2x1, costs no aspects and matches the bookshelf transformation contract.

steps: Pan one category, switch categories, close and reopen the book.
tests.expected: Selected category and one shared map pan persist as in GuiResearchBrowser; categories do not keep separate pans.

steps: Open book, change category and turn pages.
tests.expected: Open/page/category sounds use original page/cameraclack events at 1.0/0.66/0.4 volume and pitch 1.0.

steps: Open odd/even page counts and use previous/next arrows.
tests.expected: Left page is always even, navigation moves by two and clamps to the final valid spread.

steps: Click an item/recipe reference, follow another reference, then use center back.
tests.expected: Completed original research pages resolve, static history unwinds one entry at a time, and center back appears only with history.

steps: Press Escape or inventory while reading a research spread.
tests.expected: Book returns to research browser and clears recipe-reference history rather than closing to gameplay.

steps: Scroll mouse wheel over browser and research pages.
tests.expected: Wheel neither pans research map nor turns pages.

steps: Two clients open normal/Cheat books and request primary/secondary research concurrently.
tests.expected: Server owns progression, costs and note creation; each player receives independent synchronized research/aspect state.

steps: Reopen book repeatedly and change categories within one client session.
tests.expected: Static selected category/shared pan survive screen recreation without writing item NBT or world data.

steps: Compare normal/Cheat item textures and browser/book GUI textures to TC4 4.2.3.5.
tests.expected: All retained original resources match byte-for-byte and use the original 256/512 texture geometry.

steps: Inspect wizard-tower and seven legacy chest-pool acquisition integration.
tests.expected: Exact count/weight contracts are present; missing original structure/global chest adapters remain explicitly blocked and are not approximated.

steps: Join with a new player and inspect all six primal discoveries and pools.
tests.expected: All primals are known and each starts at zero research points.

steps: Load a player with both ThaumcraftAspectKnowledge and ThaumcraftOriginalAspectWallet amounts.
tests.expected: Each primal uses the larger stored amount, never a sum, and the legacy wallet is removed.

steps: Enumerate normal research progression entries.
tests.expected: Exactly the 201 TC4 4.2.3.5 entries participate; rebuild/addon entries are excluded.

steps: Create notes for one-tag and multi-tag original researches.
tests.expected: The note contains exactly the research tag aspects with no inferred supplementary anchors.

steps: Request a note while an inventory note with the same research key already exists.
tests.expected: The existing note is selected and no paper or ink is consumed.

steps: Create a primary research note in creative with paper and inked scribing tools.
tests.expected: One paper and one ink use are consumed exactly as in survival.

steps: Place and clear aspects on a note while in creative.
tests.expected: Every edit still consumes ink and relevant aspect points/bonus sources.

steps: Send the legacy table create-note action.
tests.expected: No note is created and no invented chat is shown; notes originate from Thaumonomicon entries.

steps: Send the legacy table complete-note action with a solved note inserted.
tests.expected: The table does not learn the research; the removed solved note must be used by the player.

steps: With RESEARCHDUPE, copy a completed note repeatedly beyond its item max stack size.
tests.expected: Paper, ink sac and exact tags+copies are consumed and the note stack grows above one as in TC4.

steps: Purchase several secondary researches with exact, extra and insufficient pools.
tests.expected: Only exact ResearchItem tag amounts are consumed; no inferred aspect costs are added.

steps: Use a solved discovery with eligible sibling researches.
tests.expected: Target and eligible siblings complete silently with original side effects and only tc.researcherror on failed prerequisites.

steps: Open the table with scribing tools and a generated note; compare layout and icons to TC4.
tests.expected: Original GUI, note textures and all aspect icons render with original geometry and no second note screen.

steps: Two players independently combine aspects, edit notes, purchase and complete researches.
tests.expected: Server owns wallets, notes and progression; costs and sync remain isolated per player.

steps: Gain aspects/research, die, reconnect and clone player data.
tests.expected: Unified aspect discovery/pool and research progression copy once with no legacy-wallet duplication.

steps: Hash retained aspect icons, research table GUI and research-note textures against TC4 4.2.3.5.
tests.expected: All retained original assets match byte-for-byte.

steps: Build exact 3x3x3 obsidian/nether-brick/lava/fence structure and use researched wand.
tests.expected: Structure transforms once, costs 50 Ignis + 50 Terra and exposes the correct nozzle.

steps: Alter one shell block or add a second fence and use the wand.
tests.expected: No blocks or vis are consumed.

steps: Drop smeltable and unsmeltable item entities into the core.
tests.expected: Smeltables enter the 32-slot inventory; invalid items fizz and are destroyed.

steps: Measure smelting with 0-3 active bellows and with/without speed credit.
tests.expected: Cook times are 140/120/100/80 or 80/60/40/20 ticks.

steps: Connect energized Ignis vis and smelt items.
tests.expected: Furnace drains up to 5 centivis when speed credit reaches zero.

steps: Connect an Ignis tube to the outward nozzle face.
tests.expected: Nozzle advertises Ignis suction 128 below 40 ticks and adds 600 ticks per accepted essentia.

steps: Place bellows two blocks away, orient toward core, then power/unpower them.
tests.expected: At most three unpowered correctly oriented bellows reduce cook time by 20 ticks each.

steps: Smelt supported ores, clusters and raw meats repeatedly.
tests.expected: All 18 TC4 bonus mappings and 25%/44%-per-bellows probabilities apply.

steps: Smelt recipes with known output count and experience.
tests.expected: Items and vanilla-rounded XP eject through nozzle with original position and velocity.

steps: Stand a non-fire-immune living entity in the core.
tests.expected: Entity takes 3 lava damage and burns for 10 seconds.

steps: Break any formed shell part and separately break the core.
tests.expected: Entire 3x3x3 restores to original blocks and core destruction spawns the buffed Blaze.

steps: Save/reload partially cooking furnace with items and speed credit.
tests.expected: Items/Slot/CookTime/SpeedyTime reload exactly and cooking resumes.

steps: Run formation, intake, smelting and dismantling on dedicated server.
tests.expected: All mutation remains server-authoritative with no duplicated output or Blaze.

steps: Observe shell, core, nozzle, smoke, lava-pop and output effects.
tests.expected: 132 block states use retained TC4 furnace textures and effects appear once.

steps: Hash furnace0..26 retained textures against TC4 source assets.
tests.expected: Every available original furnace texture is byte-identical.

steps: Operate furnace for an extended batch and inspect aura/flux.
tests.expected: No flux is created because TC4 TileArcaneFurnace 4.2.3.5 contains no flux mutation path.

steps: Place the Growth Lamp on each face of non-air supports, including non-sturdy glass panes.
tests.expected: The lamp accepts any non-air support and drops only when that support becomes air.

steps: Feed Herba directly from an adjacent compatible essentia source on the mounted face.
tests.expected: One Herba is drawn every fifth eligible attempt, creating 100 charges and then one 100-charge reserve.

steps: Connect a tube to the mounted face and supply Herba under lower competing suction.
tests.expected: The lamp draws only Herba from its facing side with suction 128 while depleted or lacking reserve.

steps: Observe an empty lamp through primary refill, reserve refill, depletion and reserve promotion.
tests.expected: Charges follow -1/100/0/-1 transitions and reserve promotes to 100 before another network draw.

steps: Populate plants throughout the 13 by 13 column field and run a complete scan cycle.
tests.expected: Exactly 169 column tops are shuffled and one column is scanned from y+6 down to y-6 per lamp tick.

steps: Place eligible plants at squared distances 35 and 36 from the lamp.
tests.expected: Distance 35 is eligible while the radius boundary at squared distance 36 is excluded.

steps: Measure growth attempts on crops with probabilistic random-tick behavior.
tests.expected: Each selected plant receives one natural random tick rather than guaranteed bonemeal growth.

steps: Test mature crops, cocoa, nether wart, stacked cactus/reeds and age-7 Mana Pods.
tests.expected: Original CropUtils-grown targets are skipped without consuming a charge.

steps: Add a plant block to thaumcraft:growth_lamp_blacklist and place it in range.
tests.expected: Blacklisted plants never receive a growth tick and do not consume charges.

steps: Deplete and refill the lamp while measuring emitted block light.
tests.expected: Inactive light is 8 and active light is 15 with matching on/off body textures.

steps: Place the lamp in all six orientations and inspect the Bore nozzle.
tests.expected: The retained Bore nozzle uses the original six transformations and texture.

steps: Mount the lamp directly on an Arcane Bore Base in every orientation.
tests.expected: A second opposing connector is rendered exactly as in the shared TC4 lamp renderer.

steps: Allow a selected crop state to change with a player within and beyond 32 blocks.
tests.expected: Seven green sparkles using color 4259648 are sent only when the remembered state changes and a player is nearby.

steps: Save and reload lamps with facing, reserve and partial charges.
tests.expected: Only orientation, reserve and charges persist; transient scan/draw counters rebuild safely.

steps: Run essentia intake, target selection and growth on a dedicated server with two clients.
tests.expected: Charges and world mutation occur once on the server with synchronized light and effects.

steps: Exercise Growth and Fertility lamps side by side after the shared block/renderer update.
tests.expected: Growth uses Herba and plant logic while Fertility behavior remains separate and is not claimed closed by this release.

steps: Mount the Fertility Lamp on glass panes and each of the six faces, then remove the support.
tests.expected: Any non-air support is accepted and the lamp drops only when that support becomes air.

steps: Attach the lamp directly to the top of a Victus jar and to an essentia tube.
tests.expected: One Victus is drawn every fifth eligible tick only through the mounted connector.

steps: Feed more than four Victus into an idle Fertility Lamp.
tests.expected: Charges stop at four and suction follows 128 - charges*10.

steps: Load a lamp with two charges and two eligible animals.
tests.expected: The first breeding scan occurs immediately because the transient counter starts at zero.

steps: Keep eligible animals in range for 600 server ticks.
tests.expected: Breeding scans repeat every 300 charged ticks and not while charges are below two.

steps: Mix adult animals of different Java classes inside the radius.
tests.expected: Only two adults of the exact same class are placed in love mode.

steps: Test groups of seven and eight exact-class animals.
tests.expected: A group of seven may be affected; a group of eight or more is ignored.

steps: Include babies, already-in-love animals and eligible adults.
tests.expected: Only age-zero adults not already in love are candidates.

steps: Trigger one successful pair with exactly two charges.
tests.expected: Exactly two charges are consumed and both animals enter love mode.

steps: Toggle charges between zero and positive values.
tests.expected: Light and body texture switch between 8/off and 15/active.

steps: Place the lamp on all six faces and on an Arcane Bore Base.
tests.expected: The Bore-textured nozzle follows the connector and the base shows the opposing second connector.

steps: Save and reload facing and partial charges.
tests.expected: Only orientation and charges persist; draw and breeding counters restart transiently.

steps: Craft the Fertility Lamp through infusion after LAMPFERTILITY.
tests.expected: Catalyst, six components, instability four and Bestia/Victus/Lux costs match TC4.

steps: Unlock the hidden LAMPFERTILITY research from its parents and aspect triggers.
tests.expected: Coordinates, parents, triggers, aspects, page and recipe link match TC4.

steps: Run jar intake and animal pairing with two clients connected.
tests.expected: Essentia and breeding mutate once on the server and synchronize light/state to both clients.

steps: Run Growth and Fertility lamps side by side on jars and tubes.
tests.expected: Growth remains Herba/reserve/plant logic while Fertility remains Victus/four-charge/animal logic.

steps: Place a Thaumcraft table and right-click it with a normal wand.
tests.expected: The table becomes an Arcane Workbench, the wand moves to slot 10, and no vis is consumed.

steps: Place a Thaumcraft table and right-click it with a staff.
tests.expected: The table transforms, but the staff remains in the player hand and slot 10 stays empty.

steps: Put a vanilla 3x3 recipe into the workbench while a wand is installed.
tests.expected: The vanilla CraftingManager result wins before any arcane recipe lookup.

steps: Craft an arcane recipe whose original AspectList is empty.
tests.expected: The recipe consumes zero vis; no synthetic Ordo charge is applied.

steps: Craft a multi-aspect recipe with known wand discounts.
tests.expected: Each aspect is converted to centivis and discounted exactly once before affordability and consumption.

steps: Shift-click output, wand, player inventory and hotbar stacks.
tests.expected: Ranges and wand-only insertion match ContainerArcaneWorkbench indices 0..46.

steps: Craft with buckets or another container-item ingredient.
tests.expected: Every occupied grid cell loses one item and original crafting remainders return to slot, inventory or world.

steps: Attach hoppers to every face and try insertion/extraction.
tests.expected: Every face exposes only slot 10; non-staff wands insert and slot 10 extracts, while grid/output remain inaccessible.

steps: Save and reload a workbench containing grid items, output and a wand.
tests.expected: The original Inventory list restores exactly slots 0..10; old hidden slot 11 is migrated once and never saved again.

steps: Insert a wand and inspect the world renderer from several angles.
tests.expected: A single-count wand renders at 0.65/1.0625/0.25 with X 90° and Z 20° over the exact worktable model.

steps: Open the GUI with affordable and unaffordable arcane recipes.
tests.expected: 190x234 texture, slots, six aspect positions, dim ghost and Insufficient vis behavior match TC4.

steps: Craft and transform the table with two connected clients.
tests.expected: Inventory, vis, output and renderer state mutate once on the server and synchronize to both clients.

steps: Open the Thaumcraft creative tab and inspect casting-wand subtypes.
tests.expected: Exactly four fully charged representatives appear: wood/iron, greatwood/gold, silverwood/thaumium and its sceptre.

steps: Craft and inspect normal wand, staff and sceptre NBT.
tests.expected: Component identity is stored only as original root rod/cap strings; sceptre uses the root sceptre byte.

steps: Load a pre-v11.64.32 wand containing nested Wand/Rod/Cap data.
tests.expected: The stack migrates once to root rod/cap tags and the nested adapter is removed without losing vis or focus data.

steps: Equip each staff rod and compare a normal wand in the main hand.
tests.expected: Every staff grants exactly +6 attack damage; non-staff wands grant none.

steps: Switch client language and inspect wand, staff and sceptre names.
tests.expected: Cap, base rod and object names resolve through localization components in the original TC4 order.

steps: Try stacking casting wands and inspect rarity.
tests.expected: Casting wands have maximum stack size 1 and UNCOMMON rarity.

steps: Hold each elemental wand below 10% matching primal vis for 200 ticks.
tests.expected: Exactly one displayed vis (100 centivis) is restored on each eligible cadence.

steps: Hold a primal staff with several primal aspects below 10% for repeated 50-tick intervals.
tests.expected: One eligible primal aspect is selected with world RNG and gains exactly one displayed vis per interval.

steps: Measure crafting cost with copper and silver caps across all primal aspects.
tests.expected: Copper uses 1.0 for Ordo/Perditio and 1.1 otherwise; silver uses 0.95 for Aer/Terra/Ignis/Aqua and 1.0 otherwise.

steps: Assemble a sceptre from several rod/cap pairs and measure capacity and cost.
tests.expected: Capacity is rod capacity x150 centivis and cap modifier receives the original -0.1 sceptre adjustment, floored at 0.1.

steps: Inspect wand rod and cap component items in creative and JEI.
tests.expected: The de-metadata catalogue exposes 17 original rod/staff variants and 9 cap variants; duplicate migration aliases and wooden_wand_core stay hidden.

steps: Inspect JEI wand assembly results with different vis and focus NBT.
tests.expected: Subtype identity depends only on root rod, cap and sceptre state; vis and focus data do not split otherwise identical results.

steps: Craft, migrate and equip rods/caps on a dedicated server with two clients.
tests.expected: Root NBT, creative representative data, staff attributes and vis regeneration mutate on the server and synchronize consistently.

steps: Install an upgraded focus on a wand, save and reload it.
tests.expected: The wand stores one complete focus ItemStack in lower-case focus NBT and preserves all five upgrade ranks.

steps: Load a pre-v11.64.33 wand containing the temporary upper-case Focus string.
tests.expected: The id migrates once to a canonical lower-case focus ItemStack compound and the old string is removed.

steps: Place upgraded foci into several of the 18 pouch slots, save and reload.
tests.expected: Only Inventory list entries with byte Slot and complete ItemStack NBT are persisted; SelectedFocus is absent.

steps: Open the Focus Pouch from main hand and off hand and move foci with clicks and shift-click.
tests.expected: The original 18-slot 6x3 layout opens, accepts only foci, protects the held pouch slot and preserves slot contents.

steps: Hold F with a non-sceptre casting wand and several foci in inventory and pouches.
tests.expected: The mouse is released and the original radial/radial2 textures display the sorted focus icons around the installed focus.

steps: Hover a focus in the F radial and release F.
tests.expected: Exactly the hovered sorting-helper key is sent once and the server swaps the focus with camera tick pitch 1.0.

steps: Press Shift+F with an installed focus.
tests.expected: The radial does not open; REMOVE is sent once and successful storage plays camera tick pitch 0.9.

steps: Place a wand only in off hand and press F; repeat with a main-hand wand.
tests.expected: Focus switching applies only to the main-hand non-sceptre wand.

steps: Right-click a wand while holding a focus or Focus Pouch in the off hand.
tests.expected: No focus is installed, removed or cycled; right-click remains the installed focus cast/use path.

steps: Provide all ten foci with varied five-rank upgrade NBT in loose inventory and pouches.
tests.expected: Radial and server selection use the same original prefix plus five signed upgrade ids in TreeMap order.

steps: Fill inventory and all pouches, then request a different focus.
tests.expected: If the installed focus cannot be returned, the new focus is restored to its exact source slot and the wand remains unchanged.

steps: Two clients observe one player changing and removing upgraded foci.
tests.expected: The server owns inventory/NBT mutation and all clients receive the same installed focus stack and upgrade data.

steps: Use radial selection and Shift+F on a dedicated server with inventory and off-hand pouch sources.
tests.expected: Packet handling rejects sceptres/off-hand-only wands and invokes WandManager.changeFocus for the main-hand wand.

steps: Inspect all ten focus icons, wand ornaments/depth layers, pouch GUI and radial selector.
tests.expected: Original TC4 textures are used without approximate replacements and upgraded focus NBT remains visually identifiable.

steps: Build the original 3x3x3 raw altar and use a researched wand.
tests.expected: The matrix, four arcane-stone middle corners, four arcane-brick lower corners, center pedestal and 17 air cells are recognized.

steps: Attempt altar conversion before and after completing INFUSION research.
tests.expected: Only the researched player can convert the raw altar.

steps: Convert with exactly 25 vis in each primal, then repeat with one aspect short.
tests.expected: Exactly 25 of each primal is consumed atomically; a short aspect consumes nothing.

steps: Inspect all four converted pillars and break both halves.
tests.expected: Four original orientations are used; lower halves drop arcane stone bricks and upper halves drop arcane stone.

steps: Place and remove a stack through the arcane pedestal.
tests.expected: Only one item is stored; interaction, sounds and entity drop follow TC4.

steps: Read comparator output with empty and occupied pedestal.
tests.expected: Output is 0 when empty and 15 when occupied.

steps: Save and reload an occupied pedestal.
tests.expected: The item survives under Items with Slot byte 0 and old Stored data migrates once.

steps: Use a wand on a valid inactive formed altar.
tests.expected: The matrix activates; a second use with catalyst/components starts crafting.

steps: Remove a pillar while idle and while crafting.
tests.expected: Validity is checked every 100 idle ticks and every 20 crafting ticks; a running craft pauses without losing locked state.

steps: Observe normal and enchantment XP craft phases.
tests.expected: craftCycle uses global count modulo 10, switching to 20 during XP drain and back to 10 afterward.

steps: Observe one component after source FX starts.
tests.expected: itemCount starts at five and the item is consumed only on the fifth later craftCycle pass.

steps: Place two pedestals at different heights in one x/z column.
tests.expected: Only the first pedestal found while scanning downward is cached.

steps: Place pedestals and stabilizers at boundary and out-of-bound coordinates.
tests.expected: Pedestals use radius 8/y -1..-10; stabilizers use radius 12/y +5..-10.

steps: Break the matrix during a running craft.
tests.expected: A block-damaging explosion of strength 2.0 occurs; idle destruction does not explode.

steps: Observe inactive, active and crafting matrix states.
tests.expected: Original cubelet startup, active glow, wobble and crafting halo render without server-state mutation.

steps: Observe item and block stacks on a pedestal with fancy graphics on/off.
tests.expected: Original y=1.15 bob/rotation/scales render, including the second flat-item copy when fancy graphics is disabled.

steps: Two clients activate and run one altar on a dedicated server.
tests.expected: Only the server mutates vis, pedestal contents, recipe state and NBT; both clients receive matching updates.

steps: Reload during XP, essentia and component travel phases.
tests.expected: The locked catalyst, recipe and pending costs survive; transient countDelay, itemCount, cached pedestals and source FX restart from original field defaults without duplication or refund.

steps: Fill unlabelled and labelled normal jars from above.
tests.expected: Capacity is 64; suction is 32 unlabelled, 64 labelled below capacity and 0 when full.

steps: Fill labelled and unlabelled Void Jars past 64.
tests.expected: Compatible excess is destroyed; displayed amount remains 64; full suction is 32 and labelled suction is 48 only below capacity.

steps: Connect tubes to every jar face.
tests.expected: Only the UP face exposes connection, input and output.

steps: Provide one-point essentia above a jar and observe server ticks.
tests.expected: The jar attempts exactly one pull every five ticks using the original suction comparison.

steps: Apply an aspect label, then sneak-click its visible face.
tests.expected: The jar locks to the aspect; removal clears the filter and drops one blank label with the page sound.

steps: Sneak-use an empty hand on labelled and unlabelled filled jars.
tests.expected: Contents are cleared; a label retains the zero-amount type, while an unlabelled jar becomes untyped.

steps: Transfer phials at 7/8 and 56/57 jar thresholds.
tests.expected: Transfers occur only in complete eight-point units and container items are returned atomically.

steps: Save and reload a labelled jar.
tests.expected: Tile NBT uses Aspect, AspectFilter, short Amount and byte facing only.

steps: Break, carry and replace a filled labelled jar.
tests.expected: Item NBT uses root Aspects[{key,amount}] plus AspectFilter; no BlockEntityTag or facing is written.

steps: Load pre-11.64.35 BlockEntityTag/Aspects and filtered_essentia_jar data.
tests.expected: Data migrates once to canonical normal/void jar state without item loss; future saves use original NBT.

steps: Run Hover Harness fuel consumption with a labelled Potentia jar.
tests.expected: Fuel decrements without rewriting the jar into legacy NBT or losing its label.

steps: Observe normal and void jars at multiple fill levels and facings.
tests.expected: Original liquid bounds, animated glow, label card, aspect icon and hash-based crooked rotation render correctly.

steps: View filled and labelled jar items in GUI, hand, ground and item frames.
tests.expected: BEWLR renders the correct shell, liquid level and label from root item NBT.

steps: Open creative and JEI, then load an old filtered_essentia_jar stack.
tests.expected: The migration alias is hidden and has no recipe; old data drops/clones as a normal labelled jar.

steps: Connect a normal tube to labelled and unlabelled jars and tick the network.
tests.expected: Suction propagates only from direct neighbours, dropping by one per normal tube.

steps: Insert a restricted tube between a source and destination.
tests.expected: Each restricted tube halves adjacent suction using integer division.

steps: Apply a typed jar label to a filtered tube, then try a phial and a mismatched aspect.
tests.expected: Only a typed label configures the filter; phials do not, and mismatched essentia is blocked.

steps: Shift-right-click a configured filtered tube with an empty hand.
tests.expected: The filter clears and one blank jar label is dropped.

steps: Rotate a one-way tube through each facing and test suction and transfer.
tests.expected: Suction enters only from facing opposite while transfer excludes that input side.

steps: Toggle a valve manually, then apply and remove redstone power.
tests.expected: Manual and redstone transitions update allowFlow with original squeek sound and without deleting topology.

steps: Close a valve containing one essentia and pull from an adjacent stronger tube.
tests.expected: The trapped unit remains drainable from an open side although the valve no longer propagates suction.

steps: Fill a buffer with several aspects.
tests.expected: The mixed buffer caps at eight total points and exposes a random stored aspect when queried.

steps: Shift-wand each buffer connection through three states.
tests.expected: Choke cycles normal to blue to red to normal, producing bellows suction, 1, 0, then bellows suction.

steps: Measure comparator output for buffer amounts 0 through 8.
tests.expected: Output follows floor(amount/8*14)+1 for nonzero amounts and reaches 15 at eight.

steps: Save and reload every tube subtype.
tests.expected: Normal variants use type/amount/side/open/stype/samount, filter adds AspectFilter, valve adds flow/hadpower, and buffer uses root Aspects/open/choke only.

steps: Observe a valve opening/closing and buffer choke bands on all faces.
tests.expected: Valve rotates 20 degrees per tick with original travel; buffer choke rods are blue or red on the configured side.

steps: Toggle tube sides and rotate valves/one-way tubes.
tests.expected: Baked arms and selection geometry immediately match open sides, facing and original core/arm bounds.

steps: Run a mixed jar-filter-restrict-oneway-valve-buffer chain, restart the world, and resume.
tests.expected: Persistent state survives and transient suction/tick/vent/bellows state is recalculated without duplication or loss.

steps: Insert normal fuel and an aspect-bearing input through the GUI.
tests.expected: Fuel burns, input is consumed after the bellows-scaled cook time, and all item aspects enter the 50-point reservoir.

steps: Burn Alumentum in the furnace.
tests.expected: Fuel lasts 6400 ticks and distillation changes from 40 to 20 ticks.

steps: Place five contiguous alembics above the furnace.
tests.expected: All five are discovered; occupied matching alembics each receive one essentia before one empty alembic is selected.

steps: Apply blank and typed Jar Labels to filled and empty alembics.
tests.expected: Blank labels require a stored aspect; typed labels may type an empty alembic; incompatible essentia is rejected.

steps: Sneak-use a filtered or filled alembic.
tests.expected: Filter removal drops one blank label; an unfiltered alembic is emptied with original sounds.

steps: Use normal and Void Jars on a filled alembic.
tests.expected: Compatible normal jars fill to 64; Void Jars remove all offered essentia while retaining at most 64.

steps: Use a wand on each horizontal alembic side.
tests.expected: The selected face becomes blocked while all other non-down faces remain outputs.

steps: Insert/extract with hoppers or IItemHandler from each side.
tests.expected: Top exposes no slots, bottom exposes fuel and extracts only empty buckets, horizontal sides expose input.

steps: Save and reload a burning furnace.
tests.expected: BurnTime, Vis, speedBoost, CookTime, Items and Aspects persist; counter, bellows and smeltTime are recalculated.

steps: Save and reload filled and typed-empty alembics.
tests.expected: Lowercase aspect/amount, AspectFilter and facing survive exactly.

steps: Observe ignition and burnout.
tests.expected: Light switches between 0 and 12 and smoke/flame particles appear only while burning.

steps: Render standalone, furnace-mounted and stacked alembics.
tests.expected: Legs, TubeMain and TubeSmall match the original conditional OBJ parts and facing rotations.

steps: Render a filtered alembic connected to non-tube transports.
tests.expected: Original label/aspect icon and Bore nozzles appear on the correct faces.

steps: Restart during cooking and during a five-alembic distribution cycle.
tests.expected: Persistent contents resume without preserving the temporary distillation phase or duplicating essentia.

steps: Place a compound essentia jar directly below the centrifuge and remove redstone power.
tests.expected: Every fifth tick the centrifuge draws exactly one non-primal essentia without requiring an intermediate tube.

steps: Place a filled alembic directly below the centrifuge.
tests.expected: The centrifuge accepts one compound point from the alembic bottom connection and starts a 39-tick cycle.

steps: Stack two centrifuges and provide compound output to the lower unit.
tests.expected: The upper unit may draw the lower compound output and split it again.

steps: Offer each primal aspect from below.
tests.expected: Primal essentia is rejected and remains in the source.

steps: Process repeated samples of a compound aspect.
tests.expected: Each point becomes exactly one of its two direct components using the world RNG.

steps: Apply and remove redstone during draw and processing.
tests.expected: Power pauses suction, processing and spin; work resumes without consuming extra essentia.

steps: Probe all six essentia transport faces.
tests.expected: Only bottom is input with suction 64; only top is output; horizontal faces do not connect.

steps: Save and reload idle, processing and output-filled centrifuges.
tests.expected: Only aspectIn, aspectOut and facing persist; process, counter, rotation and rotationSpeed reset.

steps: Reload while aspectIn is present.
tests.expected: The unsaved process counter resets to zero and the stored input is split on the next unpowered server tick.

steps: Observe the placed centrifuge from all sides.
tests.expected: Top and bottom boxes remain static while only the original crossbar, dinguses and core rotate.

steps: Run and stop the centrifuge repeatedly.
tests.expected: Rotation accelerates by 2 to 20, decelerates by 0.5, and pump sound plays on each original half-turn crossing.

steps: Inspect the item in GUI, hand, ground and item frame.
tests.expected: The full six-part model is shown with the original texture and the inventory assembly does not animate.

steps: Inspect collision/selection and mine the block.
tests.expected: Shape is the original 8x16x8 column with hardness 0.5, resistance 5 and metal sound.

steps: Unlock CENTRIFUGE and craft from the Arcane Workbench.
tests.expected: Recipe uses two essentia tubes, alembic, alchemical construct and piston for 5 Aqua, 5 Ordo and 5 Perditio; research remains concealed under TUBEFILTER.

steps: Run the centrifuge while a server essentia update packet is sent to the observing client.
tests.expected: The packet updates aspectIn, aspectOut and facing without resetting transient client rotation or rotationSpeed.

steps: Place a crucible with two alchemical constructs vertically above it.
Unlock THAUMATORIUM and use a charged wand on either construct.
tests.expected: The wand atomically consumes 15 Ignis, 30 Ordo and 30 Aqua before discounts; the two constructs become the lower/upper Thaumatorium and preserve the clicked output face.

steps: Insert a catalyst, browse all unlocked matching crucible recipes and toggle output icons.
Attach and remove support-facing mnemonic matrices, then reload the world.
tests.expected: Base memory is one recipe, each oriented matrix adds two, excess recipes trim from the end, and recipe ids plus owner names survive NBT reload.

steps: Heat the crucible, connect essentia tubes and remember a matching formula.
Observe suction, block the output inventory, then free one output slot.
tests.expected: Suction is 128 and pulls one unit per five ticks; redstone pauses it; blocked output consumes nothing; completion consumes one catalyst, clears the legacy buffer, fires the owner crafting event and ejects leftovers at original velocity.

steps: Inspect the two-block model from all sides and open the GUI.
Browse recipes/aspects and observe the floating remembered output.
tests.expected: The original transformed OBJ and texture render across two blocks; GUI is 176x166 with catalyst at 48,16, output at 112,16, six-aspect scrolling, memory counter and a formula icon cycling every 40 ticks.

previous_verified_server_baseline.scope: Previous verified source state only; v11.63.60 retains all prior contracts and adds four Infusion Matrix lifecycle contracts requiring a fresh 52-test run.
